Inside the main hall, carvings—such as the sliding doors from the old shrine building—are enshrined. * Wish-Granting Stone – A stone enshrined in the inner garden of the {{ill|Evil-Avoidance Purification Gate|ja|厄除厄祓門}}, offered by devotees in prayer for the fulfillment of their wishes. * Evil-Avoidance and Evil-Purging Gate – A stone gate modeled after the {{ill|doll|ja|人形|lt=Doll|lt_ja=ひとがた}} used in the Shinto purification ritual known as {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓}}, offered by philanthropists in hopes of ensuring community prosperity. * Shrine Boat – A model of the maritime procession—the highlight of the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}}—is on display. The wall depicts the scene on the festival’s first day when the sacred spirit is transferred from the port onto the boat and then proceeds toward the “Sacred Travel Quarters” (御旅所). * Mikoshi Hall – It houses two {{ill|mikoshi|ja|神輿}} used in the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}}. * Purifying Stone Steps – The 108 stone steps leading to the main hall are called the “Purifying Stone Steps” because their number equals that of {{ill|worldly desires|ja|煩悩}}, and they are regarded as a path of {{ill|spiritual training|ja|修行}}. They are made from the finest “blue” {{ill|Komatsu Stone|ja|小松石}} produced locally, their appearance becoming especially striking when wet. * {{ill|Shrine Office|ja|社務所}} * Yoritomo's Seat Stone – According to a famous legend, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, defeated in the {{ill|Ishibashiyama Campaign|ja|石橋山合戦}} (1180), hid in the “Shitodo Cave” and set sail from the {{ill|rocky coast|ja|岩海水浴場|lt=Rocky Coast|lt_ja=岩海岸}}. The rock upon which he is said to have rested during his retreat is enshrined on these grounds as “Yoritomo's Seat Stone.” * {{ill|Memorial Monument|ja|慰霊碑}} – During the Pacific War, many locals lost their lives. Every August 15, a ceremony to commemorate the war dead and pray for peace is held before the monument, attended by bereaved families and numerous others.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} === Shrine Subsidiaries === * {{ill|Ancestral Spirit Shrine|ja|祖霊社}} – A shrine dedicated to the town’s principal parishioners and war dead; when the new main hall was built, the old shrine building was retained as this subsidiary shrine. * {{ill|Mountain Shrine|ja|山神社}} – Although it had been enshrined since ancient times within the “Ohayashi (Fish-Attached Preserved Forest)” of the Manazuru Peninsula, its remote location made worship inconvenient; thus, it was re-enshrined within these grounds in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 1 (1868). * {{ill|Dragon Shrine|ja|龍神社}} – A shrine dedicated to the sea deity, fervently revered by local fishermen who pray for safe voyages and abundant catches. * {{ill|Tashima Myōjin Shrine|ja|淡島明神}} – The Shikinaisha Kada Shrine, enshrined in Kaisō District, Wakayama Prefecture, is known as Tashima Myōjin. It is said that shrines bearing the name Tashima across the country enshrine a divided spirit from this shrine. The offering of combs and hairpins, along with needle memorial services on February 8 and doll memorial services on March 3, are distinctive aspects of this faith. * Kifune {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社}} – This shrine consolidates the Inari deities once separately enshrined here and in local households. In recent years, a divided spirit has been enshrined from Fushimi Inari Taisha in Kyoto. A Hatsu-uma ritual is held in conjunction with Setsubun on February 3 each year.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Kifune Festival (Main Festival) == [[File:貴船まつり小早船.jpg|alt=Kifune Festival Kobayabune|Thumbnail|Kifune Festival Kobayabune]] [[File:貴船神社の神輿（貴船まつり）.jpg|alt=Kifune Shrine's Mikoshi (Kifune Festival)|Thumbnail|Kifune Shrine's Mikoshi (Kifune Festival)]] {{詳細記事|貴船まつり}} Kifune Festival is the main festival of {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}}, a boat festival in which beautifully adorned small swift boats ({{ill|Kobayabune|ja|小早船}}) and mikoshi boats—decorated with elaborate floral arrangements and {{ill|fukinagashi|ja|吹き流し}}—are drawn by {{ill|oar-propelled boats|ja|櫂伝馬船|lt=Oar-propelled boat|lt_ja=櫂伝馬}} in a maritime {{ill|procession|ja|渡御}}. It is held every year on the last {{ill|Saturday|ja|土曜日}} of July and the day before, staged at Manazuru Port and various locations within {{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Ceremonies == * {{ill|New Year Festival|ja|歳旦祭}} – January 1 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} – Setsubun in February * {{ill|Prayer for a Good Harvest Festival|ja|祈年祭}} – February 17 * {{ill|Showa Day|ja|昭和の日|lt=Showa Festival|lt_ja=昭和祭}} – April 29 * Summer Purification (Nagoshi no Ōharae) – June 30 * {{ill|Main Festival|ja|例大祭}} ("{{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}}") – Last Saturday of July and the day before * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} – November 23 * {{ill|New Year's Eve Ceremony|ja|除夜祭}} – December 31 * Monthly Festival – On the 1st and 15th of every month{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == Access == * Alight at {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本}}, {{ill|Tōkaidō Line|ja|東海道線}}, and {{ill|Manazuru Station|ja|真鶴駅}}. A 15‐minute walk. * {{ill|Izu Hakone Bus|ja|伊豆箱根バス}}: Alight at the “Miyamae” bus stop. (Walk 0 minutes.) == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|和書|title=The Great Series of Japanese Geography|volume=Volume 37, New Compilation of Sagami Fudoki, 2nd Volume, Section 32, Ashigarashimo District, Volume 11|chapter=Hayakawashō, Manazuru Village, Kiminomiya Myōjin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179210/86}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社}}|publisher=Yūzankaku|date=1932-8}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/documents/29332/mokuroku.pdf|title=Kanagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Inventory|website=Kanagawa Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-6|date=2021-5-1|ref={{sfnref|神奈川県ホームページ}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/674|title=Boat Festival of Kifune Shrine|website=National Designated Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-6|ref={{sfnref|国指定文化財等データベース}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kiminomiya Faith|ja|キノミヤ信仰}} * {{ill|Kashima Dance|ja|鹿島踊り}} == External Links == * [http://kibunejinja.com/ Kifune Shrine] * [https://kibunematsuri.jp/ Manazuru Kifune Festival] ([https://kibunematsuri.jp/ Kifune Festival Promotion Headquarters]) * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/674 National Designated Cultural Properties Database (Agency for Cultural Affairs)] * [http://www.town.manazuru.kanagawa.jp/ Manazuru Town] * [https://www.manazuru.net/ Manazuru Navi (General Incorporated Association Manazuru Town Tourism Association)] {{citation needed|Kifune Shinshiya}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] Requiring Documentation]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[ja:貴船神社 (真鶴町)]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]] [[Category:mass imported shinto cult pages needing dummy edit for attribution and to be reviewed if a redirect and jawiki link reviewing]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kifune Shrine (真鶴町)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kifune Shrine (真鶴町)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:貴船神社 (真鶴町)]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruseslist|{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}{{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町}}にある'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社)|Other Kifune Shrines|Kifune Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Kifune Shrine (Manazuru) | native_name = 貴船神社 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image = [[File:Kibune jinja manaduru kaidan.JPG|250px]]
Approach stairs
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|08|59|N|139|08|46|E}}}} | address = {{JPN}}
〒259-0201
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}{{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町}}真鶴1117 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|08|59|N|139|08|46|E|}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神}} | founded = {{ill|Kampyō|ja|寛平}} 1 (889){{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社}} | shrine_rank = Former Local Shrine | annual_festival = {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}} (the last Saturday of July and the day before) }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}, and {{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町}}. It enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神}} (おおくにぬしのおおかみ), {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神}} (ことしろぬしのおおかみ), and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神}} (すくなひこなのおおかみ).{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} The main festival, {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}} (きぶねまつり), has been designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}}.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/matsuri-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} It is not an enshrinement transferred from the {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} (きふねじんじゃ) of {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} and {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}. == History == * {{ill|Kampyō|ja|寛平}} 1 ({{ill|889|ja|889}}) – It is said that Hiraio, the ancestral figure of the {{ill|Shrine family|ja|社家}} Hirai family ({{ill|Ancestor|ja|祖先}}), discovered twelve wooden statues and a {{ill|letter|ja|書状}}—which had appeared offshore near Mitsuishi ({{ill|Manazuru Cape|ja|真鶴岬}})—and built a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}}, establishing it as the {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|鎮守神|lt=鎮守|lt_ja=鎮守}} of the village and naming it Kiminomiya (貴宮) {{ill|Great Deity|ja|大明神}}.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/kibune-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 12 ({{ill|1678|ja|1678}}) – According to “Sōshū West Part, Westward Line, Manazuru Village Shogakuchō” (Documents of the Gomi Family), it is recorded that as early as then, a shipboard {{ill|prayer ritual|ja|祈祷}}—which may be regarded as the origin of the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}}—was performed at Manazuru Port.{{Cite web |title=海上渡御 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/kaijoutogyo.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 1 ({{ill|1868|ja|1868}}) – Renamed to '''Kifune Shrine'''.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 6 ({{ill|1873|ja|1873}}) {{ill|July|ja|7月}} – Designated as a local shrine ({{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社}}).{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 24 ({{ill|1891|ja|1891}}) – Designated as the {{ill|Sacred Provisions Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Sacred Provisions Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進神社}}.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 12 ({{ill|1923|ja|1923}}) {{ill|September|ja|9月}} – The shrine building was destroyed by the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 33 ({{ill|1958|ja|1958}}) {{ill|November 28|ja|11月28日}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}} was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形文化財}} by {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 51 ({{ill|1976|ja|1976}}) {{ill|October 19|ja|10月19日}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}} was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} by {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 59 ({{ill|1984|ja|1984}}) {{ill|June|ja|6月}} – Prior to the 1,100‐year cycle relocation ceremony, a {{ill|gemstone wall|ja|玉垣}} constructed from Manazuru‐produced {{ill|Komatsu Stone|ja|本小松石}} was completed.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 4 ({{ill|1992|ja|1992}}) {{ill|July|ja|7月}} – 48 kasuga {{ill|lanterns|ja|灯籠|lt=lanterns|lt_ja=灯籠}} made of {{ill|granite|ja|御影石}} were erected on both sides of the grand staircase.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996}}) {{ill|December 20|ja|12月20日}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 17 ({{ill|2005|ja|2005}}) {{ill|November 20|ja|11月20日}} – The Nishikobayabune, Kiminomiya Maru, and Kashima Dance from the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}} participated in the maritime parade of the National Sea Enrichment Festival held in the {{ill|Yokohama Minato Mirai District|ja|横浜みなとみらい地区}}, which was attended by the {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇}} and {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后}}.{{Cite web |title=小早船 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/kobayabune.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神}} * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神}}{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == Shrine Grounds == * [[File:Honden, Kibube-jinja, Manazuru 2018-01-01.jpg|Thumbnail|Main Hall]] {{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿}} – The main hall of {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} was relocated onto the current 108‐step purifying {{ill|stone steps|ja|石段}} after the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}, in order to avoid {{ill|flood damage|ja|水害}}. It is a rare shrine building nationwide, constructed with {{ill|reinforced concrete|ja|鉄筋コンクリート|lt=Reinforced|lt_ja=鉄筋}}, which was regarded at the time as the most earthquake‐resistant. Inside the main hall, carvings—such as the sliding doors from the old shrine building—are enshrined. * Wish-Granting Stone – A stone enshrined in the inner garden of the {{ill|Evil-Avoidance Purification Gate|ja|厄除厄祓門}}, offered by devotees in prayer for the fulfillment of their wishes. * Evil-Avoidance and Evil-Purging Gate – A stone gate modeled after the {{ill|doll|ja|人形|lt=Doll|lt_ja=ひとがた}} used in the Shinto purification ritual known as {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓}}, offered by philanthropists in hopes of ensuring community prosperity. * Shrine Boat – A model of the maritime procession—the highlight of the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}}—is on display. The wall depicts the scene on the festival’s first day when the sacred spirit is transferred from the port onto the boat and then proceeds toward the “Sacred Travel Quarters” (御旅所). * Mikoshi Hall – It houses two {{ill|mikoshi|ja|神輿}} used in the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}}. * Purifying Stone Steps – The 108 stone steps leading to the main hall are called the “Purifying Stone Steps” because their number equals that of {{ill|worldly desires|ja|煩悩}}, and they are regarded as a path of {{ill|spiritual training|ja|修行}}. They are made from the finest “blue” {{ill|Komatsu Stone|ja|小松石}} produced locally, their appearance becoming especially striking when wet. * {{ill|Shrine Office|ja|社務所}} * Yoritomo's Seat Stone – According to a famous legend, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, defeated in the {{ill|Ishibashiyama Campaign|ja|石橋山合戦}} (1180), hid in the “Shitodo Cave” and set sail from the {{ill|rocky coast|ja|岩海水浴場|lt=Rocky Coast|lt_ja=岩海岸}}. The rock upon which he is said to have rested during his retreat is enshrined on these grounds as “Yoritomo's Seat Stone.” * {{ill|Memorial Monument|ja|慰霊碑}} – During the Pacific War, many locals lost their lives. Every August 15, a ceremony to commemorate the war dead and pray for peace is held before the monument, attended by bereaved families and numerous others.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} === Shrine Subsidiaries === * {{ill|Ancestral Spirit Shrine|ja|祖霊社}} – A shrine dedicated to the town’s principal parishioners and war dead; when the new main hall was built, the old shrine building was retained as this subsidiary shrine. * {{ill|Mountain Shrine|ja|山神社}} – Although it had been enshrined since ancient times within the “Ohayashi (Fish-Attached Preserved Forest)” of the Manazuru Peninsula, its remote location made worship inconvenient; thus, it was re-enshrined within these grounds in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 1 (1868). * {{ill|Dragon Shrine|ja|龍神社}} – A shrine dedicated to the sea deity, fervently revered by local fishermen who pray for safe voyages and abundant catches. * {{ill|Tashima Myōjin Shrine|ja|淡島明神}} – The Shikinaisha Kada Shrine, enshrined in Kaisō District, Wakayama Prefecture, is known as Tashima Myōjin. It is said that shrines bearing the name Tashima across the country enshrine a divided spirit from this shrine. The offering of combs and hairpins, along with needle memorial services on February 8 and doll memorial services on March 3, are distinctive aspects of this faith. * Kifune {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社}} – This shrine consolidates the Inari deities once separately enshrined here and in local households. In recent years, a divided spirit has been enshrined from Fushimi Inari Taisha in Kyoto. A Hatsu-uma ritual is held in conjunction with Setsubun on February 3 each year.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Kifune Festival (Main Festival) == [[File:貴船まつり小早船.jpg|alt=Kifune Festival Kobayabune|Thumbnail|Kifune Festival Kobayabune]] [[File:貴船神社の神輿（貴船まつり）.jpg|alt=Kifune Shrine's Mikoshi (Kifune Festival)|Thumbnail|Kifune Shrine's Mikoshi (Kifune Festival)]] {{詳細記事|貴船まつり}} Kifune Festival is the main festival of {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}}, a boat festival in which beautifully adorned small swift boats ({{ill|Kobayabune|ja|小早船}}) and mikoshi boats—decorated with elaborate floral arrangements and {{ill|fukinagashi|ja|吹き流し}}—are drawn by {{ill|oar-propelled boats|ja|櫂伝馬船|lt=Oar-propelled boat|lt_ja=櫂伝馬}} in a maritime {{ill|procession|ja|渡御}}. It is held every year on the last {{ill|Saturday|ja|土曜日}} of July and the day before, staged at Manazuru Port and various locations within {{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Ceremonies == * {{ill|New Year Festival|ja|歳旦祭}} – January 1 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} – Setsubun in February * {{ill|Prayer for a Good Harvest Festival|ja|祈年祭}} – February 17 * {{ill|Showa Day|ja|昭和の日|lt=Showa Festival|lt_ja=昭和祭}} – April 29 * Summer Purification (Nagoshi no Ōharae) – June 30 * {{ill|Main Festival|ja|例大祭}} ("{{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}}") – Last Saturday of July and the day before * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} – November 23 * {{ill|New Year's Eve Ceremony|ja|除夜祭}} – December 31 * Monthly Festival – On the 1st and 15th of every month{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == Access == * Alight at {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本}}, {{ill|Tōkaidō Line|ja|東海道線}}, and {{ill|Manazuru Station|ja|真鶴駅}}. A 15‐minute walk. * {{ill|Izu Hakone Bus|ja|伊豆箱根バス}}: Alight at the “Miyamae” bus stop. (Walk 0 minutes.) == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|和書|title=The Great Series of Japanese Geography|volume=Volume 37, New Compilation of Sagami Fudoki, 2nd Volume, Section 32, Ashigarashimo District, Volume 11|chapter=Hayakawashō, Manazuru Village, Kiminomiya Myōjin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179210/86}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社}}|publisher=Yūzankaku|date=1932-8}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/documents/29332/mokuroku.pdf|title=Kanagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Inventory|website=Kanagawa Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-6|date=2021-5-1|ref={{sfnref|神奈川県ホームページ}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/674|title=Boat Festival of Kifune Shrine|website=National Designated Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-6|ref={{sfnref|国指定文化財等データベース}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kiminomiya Faith|ja|キノミヤ信仰}} * {{ill|Kashima Dance|ja|鹿島踊り}} == External Links == * [http://kibunejinja.com/ Kifune Shrine] * [https://kibunematsuri.jp/ Manazuru Kifune Festival] ([https://kibunematsuri.jp/ Kifune Festival Promotion Headquarters]) * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/674 National Designated Cultural Properties Database (Agency for Cultural Affairs)] * [http://www.town.manazuru.kanagawa.jp/ Manazuru Town] * [https://www.manazuru.net/ Manazuru Navi (General Incorporated Association Manazuru Town Tourism Association)] {{citation needed|Kifune Shinshiya}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] Requiring Documentation]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[ja:貴船神社 (真鶴町)]] [[Category:pages to be translated]] [[Category:pages to be translated]] [[Category:11]] revision.text:
{{Otheruseslist|{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}{{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町}}にある'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社)|Other Kifune Shrines|Kifune Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Kifune Shrine (Manazuru) | native_name = 貴船神社 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image = [[File:Kibune jinja manaduru kaidan.JPG|250px]]
Approach stairs
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|08|59|N|139|08|46|E}}}} | address = {{JPN}}
〒259-0201
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}{{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町}}真鶴1117 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|08|59|N|139|08|46|E|}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神}} | founded = {{ill|Kampyō|ja|寛平}} 1 (889){{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社}} | shrine_rank = Former Local Shrine | annual_festival = {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}} (the last Saturday of July and the day before) }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}, and {{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町}}. It enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神}} (おおくにぬしのおおかみ), {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神}} (ことしろぬしのおおかみ), and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神}} (すくなひこなのおおかみ).{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} The main festival, {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}} (きぶねまつり), has been designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}}.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/matsuri-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} It is not an enshrinement transferred from the {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} (きふねじんじゃ) of {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} and {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}. == History == * {{ill|Kampyō|ja|寛平}} 1 ({{ill|889|ja|889}}) – It is said that Hiraio, the ancestral figure of the {{ill|Shrine family|ja|社家}} Hirai family ({{ill|Ancestor|ja|祖先}}), discovered twelve wooden statues and a {{ill|letter|ja|書状}}—which had appeared offshore near Mitsuishi ({{ill|Manazuru Cape|ja|真鶴岬}})—and built a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}}, establishing it as the {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|鎮守神|lt=鎮守|lt_ja=鎮守}} of the village and naming it Kiminomiya (貴宮) {{ill|Great Deity|ja|大明神}}.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/kibune-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 12 ({{ill|1678|ja|1678}}) – According to “Sōshū West Part, Westward Line, Manazuru Village Shogakuchō” (Documents of the Gomi Family), it is recorded that as early as then, a shipboard {{ill|prayer ritual|ja|祈祷}}—which may be regarded as the origin of the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}}—was performed at Manazuru Port.{{Cite web |title=海上渡御 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/kaijoutogyo.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 1 ({{ill|1868|ja|1868}}) – Renamed to '''Kifune Shrine'''.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 6 ({{ill|1873|ja|1873}}) {{ill|July|ja|7月}} – Designated as a local shrine ({{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社}}).{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 24 ({{ill|1891|ja|1891}}) – Designated as the {{ill|Sacred Provisions Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Sacred Provisions Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進神社}}.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 12 ({{ill|1923|ja|1923}}) {{ill|September|ja|9月}} – The shrine building was destroyed by the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 33 ({{ill|1958|ja|1958}}) {{ill|November 28|ja|11月28日}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}} was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形文化財}} by {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 51 ({{ill|1976|ja|1976}}) {{ill|October 19|ja|10月19日}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}} was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} by {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 59 ({{ill|1984|ja|1984}}) {{ill|June|ja|6月}} – Prior to the 1,100‐year cycle relocation ceremony, a {{ill|gemstone wall|ja|玉垣}} constructed from Manazuru‐produced {{ill|Komatsu Stone|ja|本小松石}} was completed.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 4 ({{ill|1992|ja|1992}}) {{ill|July|ja|7月}} – 48 kasuga {{ill|lanterns|ja|灯籠|lt=lanterns|lt_ja=灯籠}} made of {{ill|granite|ja|御影石}} were erected on both sides of the grand staircase.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996}}) {{ill|December 20|ja|12月20日}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 17 ({{ill|2005|ja|2005}}) {{ill|November 20|ja|11月20日}} – The Nishikobayabune, Kiminomiya Maru, and Kashima Dance from the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}} participated in the maritime parade of the National Sea Enrichment Festival held in the {{ill|Yokohama Minato Mirai District|ja|横浜みなとみらい地区}}, which was attended by the {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇}} and {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后}}.{{Cite web |title=小早船 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/kobayabune.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神}} * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神}}{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == Shrine Grounds == * [[File:Honden, Kibube-jinja, Manazuru 2018-01-01.jpg|Thumbnail|Main Hall]] {{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿}} – The main hall of {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} was relocated onto the current 108‐step purifying {{ill|stone steps|ja|石段}} after the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}, in order to avoid {{ill|flood damage|ja|水害}}. It is a rare shrine building nationwide, constructed with {{ill|reinforced concrete|ja|鉄筋コンクリート|lt=Reinforced|lt_ja=鉄筋}}, which was regarded at the time as the most earthquake‐resistant. Inside the main hall, carvings—such as the sliding doors from the old shrine building—are enshrined. * Wish-Granting Stone – A stone enshrined in the inner garden of the {{ill|Evil-Avoidance Purification Gate|ja|厄除厄祓門}}, offered by devotees in prayer for the fulfillment of their wishes. * Evil-Avoidance and Evil-Purging Gate – A stone gate modeled after the {{ill|doll|ja|人形|lt=Doll|lt_ja=ひとがた}} used in the Shinto purification ritual known as {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓}}, offered by philanthropists in hopes of ensuring community prosperity. * Shrine Boat – A model of the maritime procession—the highlight of the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}}—is on display. The wall depicts the scene on the festival’s first day when the sacred spirit is transferred from the port onto the boat and then proceeds toward the “Sacred Travel Quarters” (御旅所). * Mikoshi Hall – It houses two {{ill|mikoshi|ja|神輿}} used in the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}}. * Purifying Stone Steps – The 108 stone steps leading to the main hall are called the “Purifying Stone Steps” because their number equals that of {{ill|worldly desires|ja|煩悩}}, and they are regarded as a path of {{ill|spiritual training|ja|修行}}. They are made from the finest “blue” {{ill|Komatsu Stone|ja|小松石}} produced locally, their appearance becoming especially striking when wet. * {{ill|Shrine Office|ja|社務所}} * Yoritomo's Seat Stone – According to a famous legend, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, defeated in the {{ill|Ishibashiyama Campaign|ja|石橋山合戦}} (1180), hid in the “Shitodo Cave” and set sail from the {{ill|rocky coast|ja|岩海水浴場|lt=Rocky Coast|lt_ja=岩海岸}}. The rock upon which he is said to have rested during his retreat is enshrined on these grounds as “Yoritomo's Seat Stone.” * {{ill|Memorial Monument|ja|慰霊碑}} – During the Pacific War, many locals lost their lives. Every August 15, a ceremony to commemorate the war dead and pray for peace is held before the monument, attended by bereaved families and numerous others.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} === Shrine Subsidiaries === * {{ill|Ancestral Spirit Shrine|ja|祖霊社}} – A shrine dedicated to the town’s principal parishioners and war dead; when the new main hall was built, the old shrine building was retained as this subsidiary shrine. * {{ill|Mountain Shrine|ja|山神社}} – Although it had been enshrined since ancient times within the “Ohayashi (Fish-Attached Preserved Forest)” of the Manazuru Peninsula, its remote location made worship inconvenient; thus, it was re-enshrined within these grounds in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 1 (1868). * {{ill|Dragon Shrine|ja|龍神社}} – A shrine dedicated to the sea deity, fervently revered by local fishermen who pray for safe voyages and abundant catches. * {{ill|Tashima Myōjin Shrine|ja|淡島明神}} – The Shikinaisha Kada Shrine, enshrined in Kaisō District, Wakayama Prefecture, is known as Tashima Myōjin. It is said that shrines bearing the name Tashima across the country enshrine a divided spirit from this shrine. The offering of combs and hairpins, along with needle memorial services on February 8 and doll memorial services on March 3, are distinctive aspects of this faith. * Kifune {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社}} – This shrine consolidates the Inari deities once separately enshrined here and in local households. In recent years, a divided spirit has been enshrined from Fushimi Inari Taisha in Kyoto. A Hatsu-uma ritual is held in conjunction with Setsubun on February 3 each year.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Kifune Festival (Main Festival) == [[File:貴船まつり小早船.jpg|alt=Kifune Festival Kobayabune|Thumbnail|Kifune Festival Kobayabune]] [[File:貴船神社の神輿（貴船まつり）.jpg|alt=Kifune Shrine's Mikoshi (Kifune Festival)|Thumbnail|Kifune Shrine's Mikoshi (Kifune Festival)]] {{詳細記事|貴船まつり}} Kifune Festival is the main festival of {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}}, a boat festival in which beautifully adorned small swift boats ({{ill|Kobayabune|ja|小早船}}) and mikoshi boats—decorated with elaborate floral arrangements and {{ill|fukinagashi|ja|吹き流し}}—are drawn by {{ill|oar-propelled boats|ja|櫂伝馬船|lt=Oar-propelled boat|lt_ja=櫂伝馬}} in a maritime {{ill|procession|ja|渡御}}. It is held every year on the last {{ill|Saturday|ja|土曜日}} of July and the day before, staged at Manazuru Port and various locations within {{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Ceremonies == * {{ill|New Year Festival|ja|歳旦祭}} – January 1 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} – Setsubun in February * {{ill|Prayer for a Good Harvest Festival|ja|祈年祭}} – February 17 * {{ill|Showa Day|ja|昭和の日|lt=Showa Festival|lt_ja=昭和祭}} – April 29 * Summer Purification (Nagoshi no Ōharae) – June 30 * {{ill|Main Festival|ja|例大祭}} ("{{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}}") – Last Saturday of July and the day before * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} – November 23 * {{ill|New Year's Eve Ceremony|ja|除夜祭}} – December 31 * Monthly Festival – On the 1st and 15th of every month{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == Access == * Alight at {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本}}, {{ill|Tōkaidō Line|ja|東海道線}}, and {{ill|Manazuru Station|ja|真鶴駅}}. A 15‐minute walk. * {{ill|Izu Hakone Bus|ja|伊豆箱根バス}}: Alight at the “Miyamae” bus stop. (Walk 0 minutes.) == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|和書|title=The Great Series of Japanese Geography|volume=Volume 37, New Compilation of Sagami Fudoki, 2nd Volume, Section 32, Ashigarashimo District, Volume 11|chapter=Hayakawashō, Manazuru Village, Kiminomiya Myōjin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179210/86}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社}}|publisher=Yūzankaku|date=1932-8}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/documents/29332/mokuroku.pdf|title=Kanagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Inventory|website=Kanagawa Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-6|date=2021-5-1|ref={{sfnref|神奈川県ホームページ}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/674|title=Boat Festival of Kifune Shrine|website=National Designated Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-6|ref={{sfnref|国指定文化財等データベース}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kiminomiya Faith|ja|キノミヤ信仰}} * {{ill|Kashima Dance|ja|鹿島踊り}} == External Links == * [http://kibunejinja.com/ Kifune Shrine] * [https://kibunematsuri.jp/ Manazuru Kifune Festival] ([https://kibunematsuri.jp/ Kifune Festival Promotion Headquarters]) * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/674 National Designated Cultural Properties Database (Agency for Cultural Affairs)] * [http://www.town.manazuru.kanagawa.jp/ Manazuru Town] * [https://www.manazuru.net/ Manazuru Navi (General Incorporated Association Manazuru Town Tourism Association)] {{citation needed|Kifune Shinshiya}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] Requiring Documentation]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[ja:貴船神社 (真鶴町)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kifune Shrine (真鶴町)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kifune Shrine (真鶴町)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:貴船神社 (真鶴町)]] [[Category:New]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruseslist|[[神奈川県]][[足柄下郡]][[真鶴町]]にある'''貴船神社'''|その他の貴船神社|貴船神社 (曖昧さ回避)}}{{神社 | 名称=貴船神社 | 画像=[[ファイル:Kibune jinja manaduru kaidan.JPG|250px]]
参道階段
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|marker=religious-shinto|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|08|59|N|139|08|46|E}}}} | 所在地={{JPN}}
〒259-0201
[[神奈川県]][[足柄下郡]][[真鶴町]]真鶴1117 | 位置={{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|08|59|N|139|08|46|E|}} | 祭神=[[大国主大神]]
[[事代主大神]]
[[少彦名大神]] | 創建=[[寛平]]元年（[[889年]]）{{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社}} | 社格=旧[[郷社]] | 例祭=[[貴船まつり]]（７月最終土曜日及びその前日） }}'''貴船神社'''（きぶねじんじゃ）は、[[神奈川県]][[足柄下郡]][[真鶴町]]にある[[神社]]である。 [[大国主大神]]（おおくにぬしのおおかみ）、[[事代主大神]]（ことしろぬしのおおかみ）、[[少彦名大神]]（すくなひこなのおおかみ）を祀る。{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} 例大祭は[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定される[[貴船まつり]]（きぶねまつり）。{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/matsuri-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} [[京都府]][[京都市]]の[[貴船神社]]（きふねじんじゃ）からの[[勧請]]ではない。 == 歴史 == * [[寛平]]元年（[[889年]]） － 三ツ石（[[真鶴岬]]）の沖に現れた木像12体と[[書状]]を[[社家]]平井家の[[祖先]]の平井翁が[[発見]]し、[[社殿]]を建てて村の[[鎮守神|鎮守]]とし、貴宮（きのみや）[[大明神]]と称した伝えられる。{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/kibune-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} * [[寛文]]12年（[[1678年]]） － 『相州西部西筋真鶴村書上ヶ帳』（五味家文書）によると、当時から真鶴港においては、[[貴船まつり]]の起こりと言える船中[[祈祷]]が行われていたとの記述がみられる。{{Cite web |title=海上渡御 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/kaijoutogyo.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * [[明治]]元年（[[1868年]]） － '''貴船神社'''に[[改称]]。{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * 明治6年（[[1873年]]）[[7月]] － [[郷社]]に定められる。{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * 明治24年（[[1891年]]） － [[神饌幣帛料供進社|神饌幣帛料供進神社]]に指定される。{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * [[大正]]12年（[[1923年]]）9月　－　[[関東大震災]]により社殿が倒壊。{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * [[昭和]]33年（[[1958年]]）[[11月28日]] － [[貴船まつり]]が[[神奈川県]]指定[[無形文化財]]に指定される。{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * 昭和38年（[[1963年]]）新本殿が造営される。{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * 昭和51年（[[1976年]]）[[10月19日]] － [[貴船まつり]]が[[神奈川県]]指定[[無形民俗文化財]]に指定される。{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * 昭和59年（[[1984年]]）6月 － 御鎮座壱千百年式年遷宮を前に真鶴産[[本小松石]]による[[玉垣]]が完成。{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * [[平成]]4年（[[1992年]]）7月 － 大階段両脇脇に[[御影石]]による春日[[灯籠|灯篭]]48基を建立。{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * 平成8年（[[1996年]]）[[12月20日]] － [[貴船まつり]]が[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定される。{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * 平成17年（[[2005年]]）[[11月20日]] － [[横浜みなとみらい地区]]で開催された[[全国豊かな海づくり大会]]の海上[[パレード]]に、[[貴船まつり]]の西小早船・貴宮丸及び鹿島踊りが参加。[[天皇]]・[[皇后]]が観覧した。{{Cite web |title=小早船 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/kobayabune.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} == 祭神 == * [[大国主大神]] * [[事代主大神]] * [[少彦名大神]]{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == 境内 == * [[ファイル:Honden, Kibube-jinja, Manazuru 2018-01-01.jpg|サムネイル|本殿]][[本殿]] － 貴船神社の本殿は[[関東大震災]]の後、[[水害]]を避ける為、現在の108段の清めの[[石段]]の上に遷座され、当時、震災に最も強いとされた[[鉄筋コンクリート|鉄筋]]で建立された全国でも珍しい社殿で、本殿の中には旧社殿の脇障子等の彫刻が安置されている。 * 心願成就石 － 「厄除厄祓門」内部の庭園に奉斎される石。参詣者の心願成就を祈念し、崇敬者より奉納された。 * 厄除厄払門 － 神道の浄めの神事である「大祓(おおはらえ)」に用いられる「人形(ひとがた)」が模られた石門。参詣者の隆昌発展を願うべく篤志家より奉納された。 * 御船舎 － [[貴船まつり]]の華とされる「海上渡御」の模型が展示され、壁面には祭り初日に港からご神霊を船へと移乗させ「御旅所」へ向かう様が描かれている。 * [[神輿]]舎 － [[貴船まつり]]に用いられる[[神輿]]2基を収める。 * 清めの石段 － 本殿までの108段の石段は、[[煩悩]]の数と同数である事から「清めの石段」と呼称され、[[修行]]の道のりとされる。町で産出する[[小松石]]の中でも「青」という最良石で作られており、雨に濡れた際の景観は一層おもむき深い。 * [[社務所]] * 頼朝腰掛石 － 石橋山合戦([[1180年]])に敗れた[[源頼朝]]が「しとどの窟」に身を潜め、[[岩海水浴場|岩海岸]]より船出した故事は有名であるが、敵より敗走する際に休憩したと伝えられる岩が「頼朝の腰掛石」として、当社境内に奉納されている。 * [[慰霊碑]] － 太平洋戦争では町からも多くの戦没者を出した。毎年8月15日に慰霊碑の前では「戦没者を追悼し平和を祈念する式典」が執り行われ、遺族会をはじめ多くの人達が参列する。{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} === 境内社 === * 祖霊社 － 町の氏子総代や戦没者を奉る御社で、本殿を新たに造営した際、旧社殿がそのまま存置したので、これを社殿とした。 * 山神社 － 古来より真鶴半島の「お林(魚付保安林)」内に奉斎されるが、遠隔で参拝に不便なため、[[明治]]元年（[[1868年]]）に同社の境内へと勧請。 * 龍神社 － 海神をお祀りする社。漁民の多い町においては、航海の安全と大漁を祈念する参拝者によって篤く信仰される。 * [[淡島明神]]社 － 和歌山県海草郡に鎮座する式内社・加太神社は、淡島明神と呼ばれ、各地の淡島社はこの神社より御分霊を勧請したものと伝えられる。櫛(くし)や簪(かんざし)の奉納、また2月8日の針供養、3月3日の人形供養をする事も、この信仰の特色である。 * 貴船[[稲荷]]社 － 同社に古く祀られていた稲荷社に、町の各家庭で祀られていた稲荷を合祀した社。近年、京都伏見稲荷大社より御分霊を勧請した。 毎年2月3日の節分に併せて初午の神事を行っている。{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} == 貴船まつり（例大祭） == [[ファイル:貴船まつり小早船.jpg|代替文=貴船まつりの小早船|サムネイル|貴船まつりの小早船]][[ファイル:貴船神社の神輿（貴船まつり）.jpg|代替文=貴船神社の神輿（貴船まつり）|サムネイル|貴船神社の神輿（貴船まつり）]]{{詳細記事|貴船まつり}} 貴船まつりは、'''貴船神社'''の例大祭で、華やかな花飾りや[[吹き流し]]で飾られた[[小早船]]と神輿船などが[[櫂伝馬船|櫂伝馬]]に曳かれて海上[[渡御]]する船祭り。毎年７月の最終[[土曜日]]及びその前日、真鶴港及び[[真鶴町]]内各所を舞台に行われる。[[昭和33年]]（[[1958年]]）に[[神奈川県]][[指定]][[無形文化財]]に、[[昭和51年]]（[[1976年]]）に[[神奈川県]][[指定]][[無形民俗文化財]]に、[[平成8年]]（[[1996年]]）に[[重要無形民俗文化財]]に指定（指定名称：貴船神社の船祭り）。[[広島県]][[廿日市市]][[厳島|宮島町]]の[[厳島神社]]で実施される[[管絃祭]]、[[宮城県]][[塩竈市]]の[[鹽竈神社]]・[[志波彦神社]]で実施される[[塩竈みなと祭]]とあわせ、[[日本三大一覧|日本三大船祭り]]とされている。{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} == 祭礼 == * [[歳旦祭]]（さいんたんさい）　　　　　－　1月1日 * [[節分祭]]（せつぶんさい）　　　　　　－　2月節分 * [[祈年祭]]（きねんさい）　　　　　　　－　2月17日 * [[昭和の日|昭和祭]]（しょうわさい）　　　　　　－　4月29日 * 夏越の[[大祓]]（なごしのおおはらえ）　－　6月30日 * [[例大祭]]（れいたいさい）「[[貴船まつり]]（きぶねまつり）」－　7月最終土曜日及びその前日 * [[新嘗祭]]（にいなめさい）　　　　　　－　11月23日 * [[除夜]]祭（じょやさい）　　　　　　　－　12月31日 * 月次祭（つきなみさい）　　　　　　－　毎月1日、15日{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == アクセス == * [[JR東日本]][[東海道線]][[真鶴駅]]下車。徒歩15分。 * [[伊豆箱根バス]] : バス停「宮前」下車。徒歩0分。 == 脚注 == {{reflist}} == 参考文献 == * {{cite book|和書|title=大日本地誌大系|volume=第37巻[[新編相模国風土記稿]]2巻之32村里部足柄下郡巻之11|chapter=早川庄 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社|id={{NDLJP|1179210/86}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社}}|publisher=雄山閣|date=1932-8}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://web.archive.org/web/20210818161539/https://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/documents/29332/mokuroku.pdf|title=神奈川県文化財目録|website=神奈川県|accessdate=2021-11-6|date=2021-5-1|ref={{sfnref|神奈川県ホームページ}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/674|title=貴船神社の船祭り|website=国指定文化財等データベース|accessdate=2021-11-6|ref={{sfnref|国指定文化財等データベース}}}} == 関連項目 == *[[キノミヤ信仰]] *[[鹿島踊り]] == 外部リンク == * [http://kibunejinja.com/ 貴船神社] * [https://kibunematsuri.jp/ 真鶴貴船まつり]（[https://kibunematsuri.jp/ 貴船まつり推進本部]） * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/674 国指定文化財等データベース（文化庁）] * [http://www.town.manazuru.kanagawa.jp/ 真鶴町] * [https://www.manazuru.net/ 真鶴ナビ（一般社団法人真鶴町観光協会）] {{DEFAULTSORT:きふねしんしや}} [[Category:神奈川県の神社]] [[Category:神奈川県の旧郷社]] [[Category:真鶴町の建築物]] [[Category:真鶴町の歴史]] [[Category:貴船神社]] [[Category:記録作成等の措置を講ずべき無形の民俗文化財]] [[Category:重要無形民俗文化財]]

{{Otheruseslist|{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}{{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町}}にある'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社)|Other Kifune Shrines|Kifune Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Kifune Shrine (Manazuru) | native_name = 貴船神社 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image = [[File:Kibune jinja manaduru kaidan.JPG|250px]]
Approach stairs
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|08|59|N|139|08|46|E}}}} | address = {{JPN}}
〒259-0201
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}{{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町}}真鶴1117 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|08|59|N|139|08|46|E|}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神}} | founded = {{ill|Kampyō|ja|寛平}} 1 (889){{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社}} | shrine_rank = Former Local Shrine | annual_festival = {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}} (the last Saturday of July and the day before) }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}, and {{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町}}. It enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神}} (おおくにぬしのおおかみ), {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神}} (ことしろぬしのおおかみ), and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神}} (すくなひこなのおおかみ).{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} The main festival, {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}} (きぶねまつり), has been designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}}.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/matsuri-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} It is not an enshrinement transferred from the {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} (きふねじんじゃ) of {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} and {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}. == History == * {{ill|Kampyō|ja|寛平}} 1 ({{ill|889|ja|889}}) – It is said that Hiraio, the ancestral figure of the {{ill|Shrine family|ja|社家}} Hirai family ({{ill|Ancestor|ja|祖先}}), discovered twelve wooden statues and a {{ill|letter|ja|書状}}—which had appeared offshore near Mitsuishi ({{ill|Manazuru Cape|ja|真鶴岬}})—and built a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}}, establishing it as the {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|鎮守神|lt=鎮守|lt_ja=鎮守}} of the village and naming it Kiminomiya (貴宮) {{ill|Great Deity|ja|大明神}}.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/kibune-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 12 ({{ill|1678|ja|1678}}) – According to “Sōshū West Part, Westward Line, Manazuru Village Shogakuchō” (Documents of the Gomi Family), it is recorded that as early as then, a shipboard {{ill|prayer ritual|ja|祈祷}}—which may be regarded as the origin of the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}}—was performed at Manazuru Port.{{Cite web |title=海上渡御 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/kaijoutogyo.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 1 ({{ill|1868|ja|1868}}) – Renamed to '''Kifune Shrine'''.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 6 ({{ill|1873|ja|1873}}) {{ill|July|ja|7月}} – Designated as a local shrine ({{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社}}).{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 24 ({{ill|1891|ja|1891}}) – Designated as the {{ill|Sacred Provisions Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Sacred Provisions Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進神社}}.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 12 ({{ill|1923|ja|1923}}) {{ill|September|ja|9月}} – The shrine building was destroyed by the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 33 ({{ill|1958|ja|1958}}) {{ill|November 28|ja|11月28日}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}} was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形文化財}} by {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 51 ({{ill|1976|ja|1976}}) {{ill|October 19|ja|10月19日}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}} was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} by {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 59 ({{ill|1984|ja|1984}}) {{ill|June|ja|6月}} – Prior to the 1,100‐year cycle relocation ceremony, a {{ill|gemstone wall|ja|玉垣}} constructed from Manazuru‐produced {{ill|Komatsu Stone|ja|本小松石}} was completed.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 4 ({{ill|1992|ja|1992}}) {{ill|July|ja|7月}} – 48 kasuga {{ill|lanterns|ja|灯籠|lt=lanterns|lt_ja=灯籠}} made of {{ill|granite|ja|御影石}} were erected on both sides of the grand staircase.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996}}) {{ill|December 20|ja|12月20日}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 17 ({{ill|2005|ja|2005}}) {{ill|November 20|ja|11月20日}} – The Nishikobayabune, Kiminomiya Maru, and Kashima Dance from the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}} participated in the maritime parade of the National Sea Enrichment Festival held in the {{ill|Yokohama Minato Mirai District|ja|横浜みなとみらい地区}}, which was attended by the {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇}} and {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后}}.{{Cite web |title=小早船 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/kobayabune.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神}} * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神}}{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == Shrine Grounds == * [[File:Honden, Kibube-jinja, Manazuru 2018-01-01.jpg|Thumbnail|Main Hall]] {{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿}} – The main hall of {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} was relocated onto the current 108‐step purifying {{ill|stone steps|ja|石段}} after the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}, in order to avoid {{ill|flood damage|ja|水害}}. It is a rare shrine building nationwide, constructed with {{ill|reinforced concrete|ja|鉄筋コンクリート|lt=Reinforced|lt_ja=鉄筋}}, which was regarded at the time as the most earthquake‐resistant. Inside the main hall, carvings—such as the sliding doors from the old shrine building—are enshrined. * Wish-Granting Stone – A stone enshrined in the inner garden of the {{ill|Evil-Avoidance Purification Gate|ja|厄除厄祓門}}, offered by devotees in prayer for the fulfillment of their wishes. * Evil-Avoidance and Evil-Purging Gate – A stone gate modeled after the {{ill|doll|ja|人形|lt=Doll|lt_ja=ひとがた}} used in the Shinto purification ritual known as {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓}}, offered by philanthropists in hopes of ensuring community prosperity. * Shrine Boat – A model of the maritime procession—the highlight of the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}}—is on display. The wall depicts the scene on the festival’s first day when the sacred spirit is transferred from the port onto the boat and then proceeds toward the “Sacred Travel Quarters” (御旅所). * Mikoshi Hall – It houses two {{ill|mikoshi|ja|神輿}} used in the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}}. * Purifying Stone Steps – The 108 stone steps leading to the main hall are called the “Purifying Stone Steps” because their number equals that of {{ill|worldly desires|ja|煩悩}}, and they are regarded as a path of {{ill|spiritual training|ja|修行}}. They are made from the finest “blue” {{ill|Komatsu Stone|ja|小松石}} produced locally, their appearance becoming especially striking when wet. * {{ill|Shrine Office|ja|社務所}} * Yoritomo's Seat Stone – According to a famous legend, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, defeated in the {{ill|Ishibashiyama Campaign|ja|石橋山合戦}} (1180), hid in the “Shitodo Cave” and set sail from the {{ill|rocky coast|ja|岩海水浴場|lt=Rocky Coast|lt_ja=岩海岸}}. The rock upon which he is said to have rested during his retreat is enshrined on these grounds as “Yoritomo's Seat Stone.” * {{ill|Memorial Monument|ja|慰霊碑}} – During the Pacific War, many locals lost their lives. Every August 15, a ceremony to commemorate the war dead and pray for peace is held before the monument, attended by bereaved families and numerous others.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} === Shrine Subsidiaries === * {{ill|Ancestral Spirit Shrine|ja|祖霊社}} – A shrine dedicated to the town’s principal parishioners and war dead; when the new main hall was built, the old shrine building was retained as this subsidiary shrine. * {{ill|Mountain Shrine|ja|山神社}} – Although it had been enshrined since ancient times within the “Ohayashi (Fish-Attached Preserved Forest)” of the Manazuru Peninsula, its remote location made worship inconvenient; thus, it was re-enshrined within these grounds in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 1 (1868). * {{ill|Dragon Shrine|ja|龍神社}} – A shrine dedicated to the sea deity, fervently revered by local fishermen who pray for safe voyages and abundant catches. * {{ill|Tashima Myōjin Shrine|ja|淡島明神}} – The Shikinaisha Kada Shrine, enshrined in Kaisō District, Wakayama Prefecture, is known as Tashima Myōjin. It is said that shrines bearing the name Tashima across the country enshrine a divided spirit from this shrine. The offering of combs and hairpins, along with needle memorial services on February 8 and doll memorial services on March 3, are distinctive aspects of this faith. * Kifune {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社}} – This shrine consolidates the Inari deities once separately enshrined here and in local households. In recent years, a divided spirit has been enshrined from Fushimi Inari Taisha in Kyoto. A Hatsu-uma ritual is held in conjunction with Setsubun on February 3 each year.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Kifune Festival (Main Festival) == [[File:貴船まつり小早船.jpg|alt=Kifune Festival Kobayabune|Thumbnail|Kifune Festival Kobayabune]] [[File:貴船神社の神輿（貴船まつり）.jpg|alt=Kifune Shrine's Mikoshi (Kifune Festival)|Thumbnail|Kifune Shrine's Mikoshi (Kifune Festival)]] {{詳細記事|貴船まつり}} Kifune Festival is the main festival of {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}}, a boat festival in which beautifully adorned small swift boats ({{ill|Kobayabune|ja|小早船}}) and mikoshi boats—decorated with elaborate floral arrangements and {{ill|fukinagashi|ja|吹き流し}}—are drawn by {{ill|oar-propelled boats|ja|櫂伝馬船|lt=Oar-propelled boat|lt_ja=櫂伝馬}} in a maritime {{ill|procession|ja|渡御}}. It is held every year on the last {{ill|Saturday|ja|土曜日}} of July and the day before, staged at Manazuru Port and various locations within {{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Ceremonies == * {{ill|New Year Festival|ja|歳旦祭}} – January 1 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} – Setsubun in February * {{ill|Prayer for a Good Harvest Festival|ja|祈年祭}} – February 17 * {{ill|Showa Day|ja|昭和の日|lt=Showa Festival|lt_ja=昭和祭}} – April 29 * Summer Purification (Nagoshi no Ōharae) – June 30 * {{ill|Main Festival|ja|例大祭}} ("{{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}}") – Last Saturday of July and the day before * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} – November 23 * {{ill|New Year's Eve Ceremony|ja|除夜祭}} – December 31 * Monthly Festival – On the 1st and 15th of every month{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == Access == * Alight at {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本}}, {{ill|Tōkaidō Line|ja|東海道線}}, and {{ill|Manazuru Station|ja|真鶴駅}}. A 15‐minute walk. * {{ill|Izu Hakone Bus|ja|伊豆箱根バス}}: Alight at the “Miyamae” bus stop. (Walk 0 minutes.) == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|和書|title=The Great Series of Japanese Geography|volume=Volume 37, New Compilation of Sagami Fudoki, 2nd Volume, Section 32, Ashigarashimo District, Volume 11|chapter=Hayakawashō, Manazuru Village, Kiminomiya Myōjin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179210/86}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社}}|publisher=Yūzankaku|date=1932-8}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/documents/29332/mokuroku.pdf|title=Kanagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Inventory|website=Kanagawa Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-6|date=2021-5-1|ref={{sfnref|神奈川県ホームページ}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/674|title=Boat Festival of Kifune Shrine|website=National Designated Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-6|ref={{sfnref|国指定文化財等データベース}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kiminomiya Faith|ja|キノミヤ信仰}} * {{ill|Kashima Dance|ja|鹿島踊り}} == External Links == * [http://kibunejinja.com/ Kifune Shrine] * [https://kibunematsuri.jp/ Manazuru Kifune Festival] ([https://kibunematsuri.jp/ Kifune Festival Promotion Headquarters]) * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/674 National Designated Cultural Properties Database (Agency for Cultural Affairs)] * [http://www.town.manazuru.kanagawa.jp/ Manazuru Town] * [https://www.manazuru.net/ Manazuru Navi (General Incorporated Association Manazuru Town Tourism Association)] {{citation needed|Kifune Shinshiya}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] Requiring Documentation]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[ja:貴船神社 (真鶴町)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category: tier 1]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kifune Shrine (真鶴町)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kifune Shrine (真鶴町)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:貴船神社 (真鶴町)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]

{{Otheruseslist|{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}{{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町}}にある'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社)|Other Kifune Shrines|Kifune Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Kifune Shrine (Manazuru) | native_name = 貴船神社 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道}} | image = [[File:Kibune jinja manaduru kaidan.JPG|250px]]
Approach stairs
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|08|59|N|139|08|46|E}}}} | address = {{JPN}}
〒259-0201
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}{{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}{{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町}}真鶴1117 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|08|59|N|139|08|46|E|}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神}} | founded = {{ill|Kampyō|ja|寛平}} 1 (889){{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社}} | shrine_rank = Former Local Shrine | annual_festival = {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}} (the last Saturday of July and the day before) }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社}} located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡}}, and {{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町}}. It enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神}} (おおくにぬしのおおかみ), {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神}} (ことしろぬしのおおかみ), and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神}} (すくなひこなのおおかみ).{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} The main festival, {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}} (きぶねまつり), has been designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}}.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/matsuri-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} It is not an enshrinement transferred from the {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} (きふねじんじゃ) of {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府}} and {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市}}. == History == * {{ill|Kampyō|ja|寛平}} 1 ({{ill|889|ja|889}}) – It is said that Hiraio, the ancestral figure of the {{ill|Shrine family|ja|社家}} Hirai family ({{ill|Ancestor|ja|祖先}}), discovered twelve wooden statues and a {{ill|letter|ja|書状}}—which had appeared offshore near Mitsuishi ({{ill|Manazuru Cape|ja|真鶴岬}})—and built a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿}}, establishing it as the {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|鎮守神|lt=鎮守|lt_ja=鎮守}} of the village and naming it Kiminomiya (貴宮) {{ill|Great Deity|ja|大明神}}.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/kibune-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文}} 12 ({{ill|1678|ja|1678}}) – According to “Sōshū West Part, Westward Line, Manazuru Village Shogakuchō” (Documents of the Gomi Family), it is recorded that as early as then, a shipboard {{ill|prayer ritual|ja|祈祷}}—which may be regarded as the origin of the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}}—was performed at Manazuru Port.{{Cite web |title=海上渡御 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/kaijoutogyo.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 1 ({{ill|1868|ja|1868}}) – Renamed to '''Kifune Shrine'''.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 6 ({{ill|1873|ja|1873}}) {{ill|July|ja|7月}} – Designated as a local shrine ({{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社}}).{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 24 ({{ill|1891|ja|1891}}) – Designated as the {{ill|Sacred Provisions Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Sacred Provisions Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進神社}}.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正}} 12 ({{ill|1923|ja|1923}}) {{ill|September|ja|9月}} – The shrine building was destroyed by the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和}} 33 ({{ill|1958|ja|1958}}) {{ill|November 28|ja|11月28日}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}} was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形文化財}} by {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 19|ja|10月19日}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}} was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財}} by {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 4 ({{ill|1992|ja|1992}}) {{ill|July|ja|7月}} – 48 kasuga {{ill|lanterns|ja|灯籠|lt=lanterns|lt_ja=灯籠}} made of {{ill|granite|ja|御影石}} were erected on both sides of the grand staircase.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996}}) {{ill|December 20|ja|12月20日}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成}} 17 ({{ill|2005|ja|2005}}) {{ill|November 20|ja|11月20日}} – The Nishikobayabune, Kiminomiya Maru, and Kashima Dance from the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}} participated in the maritime parade of the National Sea Enrichment Festival held in the {{ill|Yokohama Minato Mirai District|ja|横浜みなとみらい地区}}, which was attended by the {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇}} and {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后}}.{{Cite web |title=小早船 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/kobayabune.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神}}}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神}} * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神}}{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == Shrine Grounds == * [[File:Honden, Kibube-jinja, Manazuru 2018-01-01.jpg|Thumbnail|Main Hall]] {{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿}} – The main hall of {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}} was relocated onto the current 108‐step purifying {{ill|stone steps|ja|石段}} after the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災}}, in order to avoid {{ill|flood damage|ja|水害}}. It is a rare shrine building nationwide, constructed with {{ill|reinforced concrete|ja|鉄筋コンクリート|lt=Reinforced|lt_ja=鉄筋}}, which was regarded at the time as the most earthquake‐resistant. Inside the main hall, carvings—such as the sliding doors from the old shrine building—are enshrined. * Wish-Granting Stone – A stone enshrined in the inner garden of the {{ill|Evil-Avoidance Purification Gate|ja|厄除厄祓門}}, offered by devotees in prayer for the fulfillment of their wishes. * Evil-Avoidance and Evil-Purging Gate – A stone gate modeled after the {{ill|doll|ja|人形|lt=Doll|lt_ja=ひとがた}} used in the Shinto purification ritual known as {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓}}, offered by philanthropists in hopes of ensuring community prosperity. * Shrine Boat – A model of the maritime procession—the highlight of the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}}—is on display. The wall depicts the scene on the festival’s first day when the sacred spirit is transferred from the port onto the boat and then proceeds toward the “Sacred Travel Quarters” (御旅所). * Mikoshi Hall – It houses two {{ill|mikoshi|ja|神輿}} used in the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}}. * Purifying Stone Steps – The 108 stone steps leading to the main hall are called the “Purifying Stone Steps” because their number equals that of {{ill|worldly desires|ja|煩悩}}, and they are regarded as a path of {{ill|spiritual training|ja|修行}}. They are made from the finest “blue” {{ill|Komatsu Stone|ja|小松石}} produced locally, their appearance becoming especially striking when wet. * {{ill|Shrine Office|ja|社務所}} * Yoritomo's Seat Stone – According to a famous legend, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝}}, defeated in the {{ill|Ishibashiyama Campaign|ja|石橋山合戦}} (1180), hid in the “Shitodo Cave” and set sail from the {{ill|rocky coast|ja|岩海水浴場|lt=Rocky Coast|lt_ja=岩海岸}}. The rock upon which he is said to have rested during his retreat is enshrined on these grounds as “Yoritomo's Seat Stone.” * {{ill|Memorial Monument|ja|慰霊碑}} – During the Pacific War, many locals lost their lives. Every August 15, a ceremony to commemorate the war dead and pray for peace is held before the monument, attended by bereaved families and numerous others.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} === Shrine Subsidiaries === * {{ill|Ancestral Spirit Shrine|ja|祖霊社}} – A shrine dedicated to the town’s principal parishioners and war dead; when the new main hall was built, the old shrine building was retained as this subsidiary shrine. * {{ill|Mountain Shrine|ja|山神社}} – Although it had been enshrined since ancient times within the “Ohayashi (Fish-Attached Preserved Forest)” of the Manazuru Peninsula, its remote location made worship inconvenient; thus, it was re-enshrined within these grounds in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治}} 1 (1868). * {{ill|Dragon Shrine|ja|龍神社}} – A shrine dedicated to the sea deity, fervently revered by local fishermen who pray for safe voyages and abundant catches. * {{ill|Tashima Myōjin Shrine|ja|淡島明神}} – The Shikinaisha Kada Shrine, enshrined in Kaisō District, Wakayama Prefecture, is known as Tashima Myōjin. It is said that shrines bearing the name Tashima across the country enshrine a divided spirit from this shrine. The offering of combs and hairpins, along with needle memorial services on February 8 and doll memorial services on March 3, are distinctive aspects of this faith. * Kifune {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社}} – This shrine consolidates the Inari deities once separately enshrined here and in local households. In recent years, a divided spirit has been enshrined from Fushimi Inari Taisha in Kyoto. A Hatsu-uma ritual is held in conjunction with Setsubun on February 3 each year.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Kifune Festival (Main Festival) == [[File:貴船まつり小早船.jpg|alt=Kifune Festival Kobayabune|Thumbnail|Kifune Festival Kobayabune]] [[File:貴船神社の神輿（貴船まつり）.jpg|alt=Kifune Shrine's Mikoshi (Kifune Festival)|Thumbnail|Kifune Shrine's Mikoshi (Kifune Festival)]] {{詳細記事|貴船まつり}} Kifune Festival is the main festival of {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社}}, a boat festival in which beautifully adorned small swift boats ({{ill|Kobayabune|ja|小早船}}) and mikoshi boats—decorated with elaborate floral arrangements and {{ill|fukinagashi|ja|吹き流し}}—are drawn by {{ill|oar-propelled boats|ja|櫂伝馬船|lt=Oar-propelled boat|lt_ja=櫂伝馬}} in a maritime {{ill|procession|ja|渡御}}. It is held every year on the last {{ill|Saturday|ja|土曜日}} of July and the day before, staged at Manazuru Port and various locations within {{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Ceremonies == * {{ill|New Year Festival|ja|歳旦祭}} – January 1 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭}} – Setsubun in February * {{ill|Prayer for a Good Harvest Festival|ja|祈年祭}} – February 17 * {{ill|Showa Day|ja|昭和の日|lt=Showa Festival|lt_ja=昭和祭}} – April 29 * Summer Purification (Nagoshi no Ōharae) – June 30 * {{ill|Main Festival|ja|例大祭}} ("{{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり}}") – Last Saturday of July and the day before * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭}} – November 23 * {{ill|New Year's Eve Ceremony|ja|除夜祭}} – December 31 * Monthly Festival – On the 1st and 15th of every month{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == Access == * Alight at {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本}}, {{ill|Tōkaidō Line|ja|東海道線}}, and {{ill|Manazuru Station|ja|真鶴駅}}. A 15‐minute walk. * {{ill|Izu Hakone Bus|ja|伊豆箱根バス}}: Alight at the “Miyamae” bus stop. (Walk 0 minutes.) == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|和書|title=The Great Series of Japanese Geography|volume=Volume 37, New Compilation of Sagami Fudoki, 2nd Volume, Section 32, Ashigarashimo District, Volume 11|chapter=Hayakawashō, Manazuru Village, Kiminomiya Myōjin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179210/86}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社}}|publisher=Yūzankaku|date=1932-8}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/documents/29332/mokuroku.pdf|title=Kanagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Inventory|website=Kanagawa Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-6|date=2021-5-1|ref={{sfnref|神奈川県ホームページ}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/674|title=Boat Festival of Kifune Shrine|website=National Designated Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-6|ref={{sfnref|国指定文化財等データベース}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kiminomiya Faith|ja|キノミヤ信仰}} * {{ill|Kashima Dance|ja|鹿島踊り}} == External Links == * [http://kibunejinja.com/ Kifune Shrine] * [https://kibunematsuri.jp/ Manazuru Kifune Festival] ([https://kibunematsuri.jp/ Kifune Festival Promotion Headquarters]) * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/674 National Designated Cultural Properties Database (Agency for Cultural Affairs)] * [http://www.town.manazuru.kanagawa.jp/ Manazuru Town] * [https://www.manazuru.net/ Manazuru Navi (General Incorporated Association Manazuru Town Tourism Association)] {{citation needed|Kifune Shinshiya}} [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] Requiring Documentation]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[ja:貴船神社 (真鶴町)]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:11]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]][[Category:qq]][[sn:Kifune Shrine (真鶴町)]][[Category:qq]] [[Category:qq]][[sn:Kifune Shrine (真鶴町)]][[Category:qq]] [[ja:貴船神社 (真鶴町)]] [[Category:New]] [[Category:New]]
