revision.text:
{{Otheruseslist|{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft}}{{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡|qq=draft}}{{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町|qq=draft}}にある'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社)|Other Kifune Shrines|Kifune Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Kifune Shrine (Manazuru) | native_name = 貴船神社 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft}} | image = [[File:Kibune jinja manaduru kaidan.JPG|250px]]
Approach stairs
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|08|59|N|139|08|46|E}}}} | address = {{JPN}}
〒259-0201
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft}}{{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡|qq=draft}}{{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町|qq=draft}}真鶴1117 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|08|59|N|139|08|46|E|}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神|qq=draft}} | founded = {{ill|Kampyō|ja|寛平|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|qq=draft}} 1 (889){{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社}} | shrine_Rank = Former Local Shrine | annual_festival = {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} (the last Saturday of July and the day before) }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町|qq=draft}}. It enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神|qq=draft}} (おおくにぬしのおおかみ), {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神|qq=draft}} (ことしろぬしのおおかみ), and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神|qq=draft}} (すくなひこなのおおかみ).{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} The main festival, {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} (きぶねまつり), has been designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/matsuri-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} It is not an enshrinement transferred from the {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社|qq=draft}} (きふねじんじゃ) of {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft}}. == History == * {{ill|Kampyō|ja|寛平|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|qq=draft}} 1 ({{ill|889|ja|889|qq=draft}}) – It is said that Hiraio, the ancestral figure of the {{ill|Shrine family|ja|社家|1=Shake (social class)|2=ja|3=社家|4=id|5=Shake (kelas sosial)|comment=Is there an actual longstanding pun comparing this to the Shakya clan of Siddartha or is that just chatGPT being wonky|lt=Shrine family|qq=draft}} Hirai family ({{ill|Ancestor|ja|祖先|qq=draft}}), discovered twelve wooden statues and a {{ill|letter|ja|書状|qq=draft}}—which had appeared offshore near Mitsuishi ({{ill|Manazuru Cape|ja|真鶴岬|qq=draft}})—and built a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft}}, establishing it as the {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|鎮守神|lt=鎮守|lt_ja=鎮守|qq=draft}} of the village and naming it Kiminomiya (貴宮) {{ill|Great Deity|ja|大明神|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/kibune-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft}} 12 ({{ill|1678|ja|1678|qq=draft}}) – According to “Sōshū West Part, Westward Line, Manazuru Village Shogakuchō” (Documents of the Gomi Family), it is recorded that as early as then, a shipboard {{ill|prayer ritual|ja|祈祷|qq=draft}}—which may be regarded as the origin of the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}}—was performed at Manazuru Port.{{Cite web |title=海上渡御 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/kaijoutogyo.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}} 1 ({{ill|1868|ja|1868|qq=draft}}) – Renamed to '''Kifune Shrine'''.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}} 6 ({{ill|1873|ja|1873|qq=draft}}) {{ill|July|ja|7月|qq=draft}} – Designated as a local shrine ({{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft}}).{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}} 24 ({{ill|1891|ja|1891|qq=draft}}) – Designated as the {{ill|Sacred Provisions Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Sacred Provisions Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft}} 12 ({{ill|1923|ja|1923|qq=draft}}) {{ill|September|ja|9月|qq=draft}} – The shrine building was destroyed by the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft}} 33 ({{ill|1958|ja|1958|qq=draft}}) {{ill|November 28|ja|11月28日|qq=draft}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形文化財|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft}} 51 ({{ill|1976|ja|1976|qq=draft}}) {{ill|October 19|ja|10月19日|qq=draft}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft}} 59 ({{ill|1984|ja|1984|qq=draft}}) {{ill|June|ja|6月|qq=draft}} – Prior to the 1,100‐year cycle relocation ceremony, a {{ill|gemstone wall|ja|玉垣|qq=draft}} constructed from Manazuru‐produced {{ill|Komatsu Stone|ja|本小松石|qq=draft}} was completed.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft}} 4 ({{ill|1992|ja|1992|qq=draft}}) {{ill|July|ja|7月|qq=draft}} – 48 kasuga {{ill|lanterns|ja|灯籠|lt=lanterns|lt_ja=灯籠|qq=draft}} made of {{ill|granite|ja|御影石|qq=draft}} were erected on both sides of the grand staircase.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft}} 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996|qq=draft}}) {{ill|December 20|ja|12月20日|qq=draft}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft}} 17 ({{ill|2005|ja|2005|qq=draft}}) {{ill|November 20|ja|11月20日|qq=draft}} – The Nishikobayabune, Kiminomiya Maru, and Kashima Dance from the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} participated in the maritime parade of the National Sea Enrichment Festival held in the {{ill|Yokohama Minato Mirai District|ja|横浜みなとみらい地区|qq=draft}}, which was attended by the {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=小早船 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/kobayabune.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft}}}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神|qq=draft}}{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == Shrine Grounds == * [[File:Honden, Kibube-jinja, Manazuru 2018-01-01.jpg|Thumbnail|Main Hall]] {{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿|qq=draft}} – The main hall of {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社|qq=draft}} was relocated onto the current 108‐step purifying {{ill|stone steps|ja|石段|qq=draft}} after the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft}}, in order to avoid {{ill|flood damage|ja|水害|qq=draft}}. It is a rare shrine building nationwide, constructed with {{ill|reinforced concrete|ja|鉄筋コンクリート|lt=Reinforced|lt_ja=鉄筋|qq=draft}}, which was regarded at the time as the most earthquake‐resistant. Inside the main hall, carvings—such as the sliding doors from the old shrine building—are enshrined. * Wish-Granting Stone – A stone enshrined in the inner garden of the {{ill|Evil-Avoidance Purification Gate|ja|厄除厄祓門|qq=draft}}, offered by devotees in prayer for the fulfillment of their wishes. * Evil-Avoidance and Evil-Purging Gate – A stone gate modeled after the {{ill|doll|ja|人形|lt=Doll|lt_ja=ひとがた|qq=draft}} used in the Shinto purification ritual known as {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓|qq=draft}}, offered by philanthropists in hopes of ensuring community prosperity. * Shrine Boat – A model of the maritime procession—the highlight of the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}}—is on display. The wall depicts the scene on the festival’s first day when the sacred spirit is transferred from the port onto the boat and then proceeds toward the “Sacred Travel Quarters” (御旅所). * Mikoshi Hall – It houses two {{ill|mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft}} used in the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}}. * Purifying Stone Steps – The 108 stone steps leading to the main hall are called the “Purifying Stone Steps” because their number equals that of {{ill|worldly desires|ja|煩悩|qq=draft}}, and they are regarded as a path of {{ill|spiritual training|ja|修行|qq=draft}}. They are made from the finest “blue” {{ill|Komatsu Stone|ja|小松石|qq=draft}} produced locally, their appearance becoming especially striking when wet. * {{ill|Shrine Office|ja|社務所|qq=draft}} * Yoritomo's Seat Stone – According to a famous legend, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft}}, defeated in the {{ill|Ishibashiyama Campaign|ja|石橋山合戦|qq=draft}} (1180), hid in the “Shitodo Cave” and set sail from the {{ill|rocky coast|ja|岩海水浴場|lt=Rocky Coast|lt_ja=岩海岸|qq=draft}}. The rock upon which he is said to have rested during his retreat is enshrined on these grounds as “Yoritomo's Seat Stone.” * {{ill|Memorial Monument|ja|慰霊碑|qq=draft}} – During the Pacific War, many locals lost their lives. Every August 15, a ceremony to commemorate the war dead and pray for peace is held before the monument, attended by bereaved families and numerous others.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} === Shrine Subsidiaries === * {{ill|Ancestral Spirit Shrine|ja|祖霊社|qq=draft}} – A shrine dedicated to the town’s principal parishioners and war dead; when the new main hall was built, the old shrine building was retained as this subsidiary shrine. * {{ill|Mountain Shrine|ja|山神社|qq=draft}} – Although it had been enshrined since ancient times within the “Ohayashi (Fish-Attached Preserved Forest)” of the Manazuru Peninsula, its remote location made worship inconvenient; thus, it was re-enshrined within these grounds in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}} 1 (1868). * {{ill|Dragon Shrine|ja|龍神社|qq=draft}} – A shrine dedicated to the sea deity, fervently revered by local fishermen who pray for safe voyages and abundant catches. * {{ill|Tashima Myōjin Shrine|ja|淡島明神|qq=draft}} – The Shikinaisha Kada Shrine, enshrined in Kaisō District, Wakayama Prefecture, is known as Tashima Myōjin. It is said that shrines bearing the name Tashima across the country enshrine a divided spirit from this shrine. The offering of combs and hairpins, along with needle memorial services on February 8 and doll memorial services on March 3, are distinctive aspects of this faith. * Kifune {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|qq=draft}} – This shrine consolidates the Inari deities once separately enshrined here and in local households. In recent years, a divided spirit has been enshrined from Fushimi Inari Taisha in Kyoto. A Hatsu-uma ritual is held in conjunction with Setsubun on February 3 each year.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Kifune Festival (Main Festival) == [[File:貴船まつり小早船.jpg|alt=Kifune Festival Kobayabune|Thumbnail|Kifune Festival Kobayabune]] [[File:貴船神社の神輿（貴船まつり）.jpg|alt=Kifune Shrine's Mikoshi (Kifune Festival)|Thumbnail|Kifune Shrine's Mikoshi (Kifune Festival)]] {{詳細記事|貴船まつり}} Kifune Festival is the main festival of {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社|qq=draft}}, a boat festival in which beautifully adorned small swift boats ({{ill|Kobayabune|ja|小早船|qq=draft}}) and mikoshi boats—decorated with elaborate floral arrangements and {{ill|fukinagashi|ja|吹き流し|qq=draft}}—are drawn by {{ill|oar-propelled boats|ja|櫂伝馬船|lt=Oar-propelled boat|lt_ja=櫂伝馬|qq=draft}} in a maritime {{ill|procession|ja|渡御|qq=draft}}. It is held every year on the last {{ill|Saturday|ja|土曜日|qq=draft}} of July and the day before, staged at Manazuru Port and various locations within {{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Ceremonies == * {{ill|New Year Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft}} – January 1 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|qq=draft}} – Setsubun in February * {{ill|Prayer for a Good Harvest Festival|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft}} – February 17 * {{ill|Showa Day|ja|昭和の日|lt=Showa Festival|lt_ja=昭和祭|qq=draft}} – April 29 * Summer Purification (Nagoshi no Ōharae) – June 30 * {{ill|Main Festival|ja|例大祭|qq=draft}} ("{{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}}") – Last Saturday of July and the day before * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft}} – November 23 * {{ill|New Year's Eve Ceremony|ja|除夜祭|qq=draft}} – December 31 * Monthly Festival – On the 1st and 15th of every month{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == Access == * Alight at {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tōkaidō Line|ja|東海道線|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Manazuru Station|ja|真鶴駅|qq=draft}}. A 15‐minute walk. * {{ill|Izu Hakone Bus|ja|伊豆箱根バス|qq=draft}}: Alight at the “Miyamae” bus stop. (Walk 0 minutes.) == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|和書|title=The Great Series of Japanese Geography|volume=Volume 37, New Compilation of Sagami Fudoki, 2nd Volume, Section 32, Ashigarashimo District, Volume 11|chapter=Hayakawashō, Manazuru Village, Kiminomiya Myōjin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179210/86}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社}}|publisher=Yūzankaku|date=1932-8}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/documents/29332/mokuroku.pdf|title=Kanagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Inventory|website=Kanagawa Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-6|date=2021-5-1|ref={{sfnref|神奈川県ホームページ}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/674|title=Boat Festival of Kifune Shrine|website=National Designated Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-6|ref={{sfnref|国指定文化財等データベース}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kiminomiya Faith|ja|キノミヤ信仰|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kashima Dance|ja|鹿島踊り|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://kibunejinja.com/ Kifune Shrine] * [https://kibunematsuri.jp/ Manazuru Kifune Festival] ([https://kibunematsuri.jp/ Kifune Festival Promotion Headquarters]) * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/674 National Designated Cultural Properties Database (Agency for Cultural Affairs)] * [http://www.town.manazuru.kanagawa.jp/ Manazuru Town] * [https://www.manazuru.net/ Manazuru Navi (General Incorporated Association Manazuru Town Tourism Association)] {{citation needed|Kifune Shinshiya}} {{translated page|ja|貴船神社 (真鶴町)|version=104285000|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Local Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Manazuru]] [[Category:History of Manazuru]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:貴船神社 (真鶴町)]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (真鶴町)]]

revision.text:
{{Otheruseslist|{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft}}{{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡|qq=draft}}{{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町|qq=draft}}にある'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社)|Other Kifune Shrines|Kifune Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Kifune Shrine (Manazuru) | native_name = 貴船神社 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Shinto|ja|神道|qq=draft}} | image = [[File:Kibune jinja manaduru kaidan.JPG|250px]]
Approach stairs
{{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=14|frame-align=center|frame-width=250|coord={{coord|35|08|59|N|139|08|46|E}}}} | address = {{JPN}}
〒259-0201
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft}}{{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡|qq=draft}}{{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町|qq=draft}}真鶴1117 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|08|59|N|139|08|46|E|}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神|qq=draft}} | founded = {{ill|Kampyō|ja|寛平|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|qq=draft}} 1 (889){{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社}} | shrine_Rank = Former Local Shrine | annual_festival = {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} (the last Saturday of July and the day before) }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町|qq=draft}}. It enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神|qq=draft}} (おおくにぬしのおおかみ), {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神|qq=draft}} (ことしろぬしのおおかみ), and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神|qq=draft}} (すくなひこなのおおかみ).{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} The main festival, {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} (きぶねまつり), has been designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/matsuri-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} It is not an enshrinement transferred from the {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社|qq=draft}} (きふねじんじゃ) of {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft}}. == History == * {{ill|Kampyō|ja|寛平|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|qq=draft}} 1 ({{ill|889|ja|889|qq=draft}}) – It is said that Hiraio, the ancestral figure of the {{ill|Shrine family|ja|社家|1=Shake (social class)|2=ja|3=社家|4=id|5=Shake (kelas sosial)|comment=Is there an actual longstanding pun comparing this to the Shakya clan of Siddartha or is that just chatGPT being wonky|lt=Shrine family|qq=draft}} Hirai family ({{ill|Ancestor|ja|祖先|qq=draft}}), discovered twelve wooden statues and a {{ill|letter|ja|書状|qq=draft}}—which had appeared offshore near Mitsuishi ({{ill|Manazuru Cape|ja|真鶴岬|qq=draft}})—and built a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft}}, establishing it as the {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|鎮守神|lt=鎮守|lt_ja=鎮守|qq=draft}} of the village and naming it Kiminomiya (貴宮) {{ill|Great Deity|ja|大明神|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/kibune-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft}} 12 ({{ill|1678|ja|1678|qq=draft}}) – According to “Sōshū West Part, Westward Line, Manazuru Village Shogakuchō” (Documents of the Gomi Family), it is recorded that as early as then, a shipboard {{ill|prayer ritual|ja|祈祷|qq=draft}}—which may be regarded as the origin of the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}}—was performed at Manazuru Port.{{Cite web |title=海上渡御 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/kaijoutogyo.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}} 1 ({{ill|1868|ja|1868|qq=draft}}) – Renamed to '''Kifune Shrine'''.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}} 6 ({{ill|1873|ja|1873|qq=draft}}) {{ill|July|ja|7月|qq=draft}} – Designated as a local shrine ({{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft}}).{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}} 24 ({{ill|1891|ja|1891|qq=draft}}) – Designated as the {{ill|Sacred Provisions Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Sacred Provisions Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft}} 12 ({{ill|1923|ja|1923|qq=draft}}) {{ill|September|ja|9月|qq=draft}} – The shrine building was destroyed by the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft}} 33 ({{ill|1958|ja|1958|qq=draft}}) {{ill|November 28|ja|11月28日|qq=draft}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形文化財|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft}} 51 ({{ill|1976|ja|1976|qq=draft}}) {{ill|October 19|ja|10月19日|qq=draft}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft}} 59 ({{ill|1984|ja|1984|qq=draft}}) {{ill|June|ja|6月|qq=draft}} – Prior to the 1,100‐year cycle relocation ceremony, a {{ill|gemstone wall|ja|玉垣|qq=draft}} constructed from Manazuru‐produced {{ill|Komatsu Stone|ja|本小松石|qq=draft}} was completed.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft}} 4 ({{ill|1992|ja|1992|qq=draft}}) {{ill|July|ja|7月|qq=draft}} – 48 kasuga {{ill|lanterns|ja|灯籠|lt=lanterns|lt_ja=灯籠|qq=draft}} made of {{ill|granite|ja|御影石|qq=draft}} were erected on both sides of the grand staircase.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft}} 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996|qq=draft}}) {{ill|December 20|ja|12月20日|qq=draft}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft}} 17 ({{ill|2005|ja|2005|qq=draft}}) {{ill|November 20|ja|11月20日|qq=draft}} – The Nishikobayabune, Kiminomiya Maru, and Kashima Dance from the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} participated in the maritime parade of the National Sea Enrichment Festival held in the {{ill|Yokohama Minato Mirai District|ja|横浜みなとみらい地区|qq=draft}}, which was attended by the {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=小早船 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/kobayabune.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft}}}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神|qq=draft}}{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == Shrine Grounds == * [[File:Honden, Kibube-jinja, Manazuru 2018-01-01.jpg|Thumbnail|Main Hall]] {{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿|qq=draft}} – The main hall of {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社|qq=draft}} was relocated onto the current 108‐step purifying {{ill|stone steps|ja|石段|qq=draft}} after the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft}}, in order to avoid {{ill|flood damage|ja|水害|qq=draft}}. It is a rare shrine building nationwide, constructed with {{ill|reinforced concrete|ja|鉄筋コンクリート|lt=Reinforced|lt_ja=鉄筋|qq=draft}}, which was regarded at the time as the most earthquake‐resistant. Inside the main hall, carvings—such as the sliding doors from the old shrine building—are enshrined. * Wish-Granting Stone – A stone enshrined in the inner garden of the {{ill|Evil-Avoidance Purification Gate|ja|厄除厄祓門|qq=draft}}, offered by devotees in prayer for the fulfillment of their wishes. * Evil-Avoidance and Evil-Purging Gate – A stone gate modeled after the {{ill|doll|ja|人形|lt=Doll|lt_ja=ひとがた|qq=draft}} used in the Shinto purification ritual known as {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓|qq=draft}}, offered by philanthropists in hopes of ensuring community prosperity. * Shrine Boat – A model of the maritime procession—the highlight of the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}}—is on display. The wall depicts the scene on the festival’s first day when the sacred spirit is transferred from the port onto the boat and then proceeds toward the “Sacred Travel Quarters” (御旅所). * Mikoshi Hall – It houses two {{ill|mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft}} used in the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}}. * Purifying Stone Steps – The 108 stone steps leading to the main hall are called the “Purifying Stone Steps” because their number equals that of {{ill|worldly desires|ja|煩悩|qq=draft}}, and they are regarded as a path of {{ill|spiritual training|ja|修行|qq=draft}}. They are made from the finest “blue” {{ill|Komatsu Stone|ja|小松石|qq=draft}} produced locally, their appearance becoming especially striking when wet. * {{ill|Shrine Office|ja|社務所|qq=draft}} * Yoritomo's Seat Stone – According to a famous legend, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft}}, defeated in the {{ill|Ishibashiyama Campaign|ja|石橋山合戦|qq=draft}} (1180), hid in the “Shitodo Cave” and set sail from the {{ill|rocky coast|ja|岩海水浴場|lt=Rocky Coast|lt_ja=岩海岸|qq=draft}}. The rock upon which he is said to have rested during his retreat is enshrined on these grounds as “Yoritomo's Seat Stone.” * {{ill|Memorial Monument|ja|慰霊碑|qq=draft}} – During the Pacific War, many locals lost their lives. Every August 15, a ceremony to commemorate the war dead and pray for peace is held before the monument, attended by bereaved families and numerous others.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} === Shrine Subsidiaries === * {{ill|Ancestral Spirit Shrine|ja|祖霊社|qq=draft}} – A shrine dedicated to the town’s principal parishioners and war dead; when the new main hall was built, the old shrine building was retained as this subsidiary shrine. * {{ill|Mountain Shrine|ja|山神社|qq=draft}} – Although it had been enshrined since ancient times within the “Ohayashi (Fish-Attached Preserved Forest)” of the Manazuru Peninsula, its remote location made worship inconvenient; thus, it was re-enshrined within these grounds in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}} 1 (1868). * {{ill|Dragon Shrine|ja|龍神社|qq=draft}} – A shrine dedicated to the sea deity, fervently revered by local fishermen who pray for safe voyages and abundant catches. * {{ill|Tashima Myōjin Shrine|ja|淡島明神|qq=draft}} – The Shikinaisha Kada Shrine, enshrined in Kaisō District, Wakayama Prefecture, is known as Tashima Myōjin. It is said that shrines bearing the name Tashima across the country enshrine a divided spirit from this shrine. The offering of combs and hairpins, along with needle memorial services on February 8 and doll memorial services on March 3, are distinctive aspects of this faith. * Kifune {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|qq=draft}} – This shrine consolidates the Inari deities once separately enshrined here and in local households. In recent years, a divided spirit has been enshrined from Fushimi Inari Taisha in Kyoto. A Hatsu-uma ritual is held in conjunction with Setsubun on February 3 each year.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Kifune Festival (Main Festival) == [[File:貴船まつり小早船.jpg|alt=Kifune Festival Kobayabune|Thumbnail|Kifune Festival Kobayabune]] [[File:貴船神社の神輿（貴船まつり）.jpg|alt=Kifune Shrine's Mikoshi (Kifune Festival)|Thumbnail|Kifune Shrine's Mikoshi (Kifune Festival)]] {{詳細記事|貴船まつり}} Kifune Festival is the main festival of {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社|qq=draft}}, a boat festival in which beautifully adorned small swift boats ({{ill|Kobayabune|ja|小早船|qq=draft}}) and mikoshi boats—decorated with elaborate floral arrangements and {{ill|fukinagashi|ja|吹き流し|qq=draft}}—are drawn by {{ill|oar-propelled boats|ja|櫂伝馬船|lt=Oar-propelled boat|lt_ja=櫂伝馬|qq=draft}} in a maritime {{ill|procession|ja|渡御|qq=draft}}. It is held every year on the last {{ill|Saturday|ja|土曜日|qq=draft}} of July and the day before, staged at Manazuru Port and various locations within {{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Ceremonies == * {{ill|New Year Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft}} – January 1 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|qq=draft}} – Setsubun in February * {{ill|Prayer for a Good Harvest Festival|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft}} – February 17 * {{ill|Showa Day|ja|昭和の日|lt=Showa Festival|lt_ja=昭和祭|qq=draft}} – April 29 * Summer Purification (Nagoshi no Ōharae) – June 30 * {{ill|Main Festival|ja|例大祭|qq=draft}} ("{{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}}") – Last Saturday of July and the day before * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft}} – November 23 * {{ill|New Year's Eve Ceremony|ja|除夜祭|qq=draft}} – December 31 * Monthly Festival – On the 1st and 15th of every month{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == Access == * Alight at {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tōkaidō Line|ja|東海道線|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Manazuru Station|ja|真鶴駅|qq=draft}}. A 15‐minute walk. * {{ill|Izu Hakone Bus|ja|伊豆箱根バス|qq=draft}}: Alight at the “Miyamae” bus stop. (Walk 0 minutes.) == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|和書|title=The Great Series of Japanese Geography|volume=Volume 37, New Compilation of Sagami Fudoki, 2nd Volume, Section 32, Ashigarashimo District, Volume 11|chapter=Hayakawashō, Manazuru Village, Kiminomiya Myōjin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179210/86}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社}}|publisher=Yūzankaku|date=1932-8}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/documents/29332/mokuroku.pdf|title=Kanagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Inventory|website=Kanagawa Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-6|date=2021-5-1|ref={{sfnref|神奈川県ホームページ}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/674|title=Boat Festival of Kifune Shrine|website=National Designated Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-6|ref={{sfnref|国指定文化財等データベース}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kiminomiya Faith|ja|キノミヤ信仰|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kashima Dance|ja|鹿島踊り|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://kibunejinja.com/ Kifune Shrine] * [https://kibunematsuri.jp/ Manazuru Kifune Festival] ([https://kibunematsuri.jp/ Kifune Festival Promotion Headquarters]) * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/674 National Designated Cultural Properties Database (Agency for Cultural Affairs)] * [http://www.town.manazuru.kanagawa.jp/ Manazuru Town] * [https://www.manazuru.net/ Manazuru Navi (General Incorporated Association Manazuru Town Tourism Association)] {{translated page|ja|貴船神社 (真鶴町)|version=104285000|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Local Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Manazuru]] [[Category:History of Manazuru]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:貴船神社 (真鶴町)]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (真鶴町)]]

〒259-0201
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft}}{{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡|qq=draft}}{{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町|qq=draft}}真鶴1117 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|08|59|N|139|08|46|E|}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神|qq=draft}} | founded = {{ill|Kampyō|ja|寛平|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|qq=draft}} 1 (889){{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社}} | shrine_Rank = Former Local Shrine | annual_festival = {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} (the last Saturday of July and the day before) }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町|qq=draft}}. It enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神|qq=draft}} (おおくにぬしのおおかみ), {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神|qq=draft}} (ことしろぬしのおおかみ), and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神|qq=draft}} (すくなひこなのおおかみ).{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} The main festival, {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} (きぶねまつり), has been designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/matsuri-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} It is not an enshrinement transferred from the {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社|qq=draft}} (きふねじんじゃ) of {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft}}. == History == * {{ill|Kampyō|ja|寛平|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|qq=draft}} 1 ({{ill|889|ja|889|qq=draft}}) – It is said that Hiraio, the ancestral figure of the {{ill|Shrine family|ja|社家|1=Shake (social class)|2=ja|3=社家|4=id|5=Shake (kelas sosial)|comment=Is there an actual longstanding pun comparing this to the Shakya clan of Siddartha or is that just chatGPT being wonky|lt=Shrine family|qq=draft}} Hirai family ({{ill|Ancestor|ja|祖先|qq=draft}}), discovered twelve wooden statues and a {{ill|letter|ja|書状|qq=draft}}—which had appeared offshore near Mitsuishi ({{ill|Manazuru Cape|ja|真鶴岬|qq=draft}})—and built a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft}}, establishing it as the {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|鎮守神|lt=鎮守|lt_ja=鎮守|qq=draft}} of the village and naming it Kiminomiya (貴宮) {{ill|Great Deity|ja|大明神|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/kibune-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft}} 12 ({{ill|1678|ja|1678|qq=draft}}) – According to “Sōshū West Part, Westward Line, Manazuru Village Shogakuchō” (Documents of the Gomi Family), it is recorded that as early as then, a shipboard {{ill|prayer ritual|ja|祈祷|qq=draft}}—which may be regarded as the origin of the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}}—was performed at Manazuru Port.{{Cite web |title=海上渡御 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/kaijoutogyo.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}} 1 ({{ill|1868|ja|1868|qq=draft}}) – Renamed to '''Kifune Shrine'''.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}} 6 ({{ill|1873|ja|1873|qq=draft}}) {{ill|July|ja|7月|qq=draft}} – Designated as a local shrine ({{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft}}).{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}} 24 ({{ill|1891|ja|1891|qq=draft}}) – Designated as the {{ill|Sacred Provisions Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Sacred Provisions Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft}} 12 ({{ill|1923|ja|1923|qq=draft}}) {{ill|September|ja|9月|qq=draft}} – The shrine building was destroyed by the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft}} 33 ({{ill|1958|ja|1958|qq=draft}}) {{ill|November 28|ja|11月28日|qq=draft}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形文化財|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft}} 51 ({{ill|1976|ja|1976|qq=draft}}) {{ill|October 19|ja|10月19日|qq=draft}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft}} 59 ({{ill|1984|ja|1984|qq=draft}}) {{ill|June|ja|6月|qq=draft}} – Prior to the 1,100‐year cycle relocation ceremony, a {{ill|gemstone wall|ja|玉垣|qq=draft}} constructed from Manazuru‐produced {{ill|Komatsu Stone|ja|本小松石|qq=draft}} was completed.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft}} 4 ({{ill|1992|ja|1992|qq=draft}}) {{ill|July|ja|7月|qq=draft}} – 48 kasuga {{ill|lanterns|ja|灯籠|lt=lanterns|lt_ja=灯籠|qq=draft}} made of {{ill|granite|ja|御影石|qq=draft}} were erected on both sides of the grand staircase.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft}} 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996|qq=draft}}) {{ill|December 20|ja|12月20日|qq=draft}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft}} 17 ({{ill|2005|ja|2005|qq=draft}}) {{ill|November 20|ja|11月20日|qq=draft}} – The Nishikobayabune, Kiminomiya Maru, and Kashima Dance from the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} participated in the maritime parade of the National Sea Enrichment Festival held in the {{ill|Yokohama Minato Mirai District|ja|横浜みなとみらい地区|qq=draft}}, which was attended by the {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=小早船 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/kobayabune.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft}}}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神|qq=draft}}{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == Shrine Grounds == * [[File:Honden, Kibube-jinja, Manazuru 2018-01-01.jpg|Thumbnail|Main Hall]] {{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿|qq=draft}} – The main hall of {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社|qq=draft}} was relocated onto the current 108‐step purifying {{ill|stone steps|ja|石段|qq=draft}} after the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft}}, in order to avoid {{ill|flood damage|ja|水害|qq=draft}}. It is a rare shrine building nationwide, constructed with {{ill|reinforced concrete|ja|鉄筋コンクリート|lt=Reinforced|lt_ja=鉄筋|qq=draft}}, which was regarded at the time as the most earthquake‐resistant. Inside the main hall, carvings—such as the sliding doors from the old shrine building—are enshrined. * Wish-Granting Stone – A stone enshrined in the inner garden of the {{ill|Evil-Avoidance Purification Gate|ja|厄除厄祓門|qq=draft}}, offered by devotees in prayer for the fulfillment of their wishes. * Evil-Avoidance and Evil-Purging Gate – A stone gate modeled after the {{ill|doll|ja|人形|lt=Doll|lt_ja=ひとがた|qq=draft}} used in the Shinto purification ritual known as {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓|qq=draft}}, offered by philanthropists in hopes of ensuring community prosperity. * Shrine Boat – A model of the maritime procession—the highlight of the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}}—is on display. The wall depicts the scene on the festival’s first day when the sacred spirit is transferred from the port onto the boat and then proceeds toward the “Sacred Travel Quarters” (御旅所). * Mikoshi Hall – It houses two {{ill|mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft}} used in the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}}. * Purifying Stone Steps – The 108 stone steps leading to the main hall are called the “Purifying Stone Steps” because their number equals that of {{ill|worldly desires|ja|煩悩|qq=draft}}, and they are regarded as a path of {{ill|spiritual training|ja|修行|qq=draft}}. They are made from the finest “blue” {{ill|Komatsu Stone|ja|小松石|qq=draft}} produced locally, their appearance becoming especially striking when wet. * {{ill|Shrine Office|ja|社務所|qq=draft}} * Yoritomo's Seat Stone – According to a famous legend, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft}}, defeated in the {{ill|Ishibashiyama Campaign|ja|石橋山合戦|qq=draft}} (1180), hid in the “Shitodo Cave” and set sail from the {{ill|rocky coast|ja|岩海水浴場|lt=Rocky Coast|lt_ja=岩海岸|qq=draft}}. The rock upon which he is said to have rested during his retreat is enshrined on these grounds as “Yoritomo's Seat Stone.” * {{ill|Memorial Monument|ja|慰霊碑|qq=draft}} – During the Pacific War, many locals lost their lives. Every August 15, a ceremony to commemorate the war dead and pray for peace is held before the monument, attended by bereaved families and numerous others.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} === Shrine Subsidiaries === * {{ill|Ancestral Spirit Shrine|ja|祖霊社|qq=draft}} – A shrine dedicated to the town’s principal parishioners and war dead; when the new main hall was built, the old shrine building was retained as this subsidiary shrine. * {{ill|Mountain Shrine|ja|山神社|qq=draft}} – Although it had been enshrined since ancient times within the “Ohayashi (Fish-Attached Preserved Forest)” of the Manazuru Peninsula, its remote location made worship inconvenient; thus, it was re-enshrined within these grounds in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}} 1 (1868). * {{ill|Dragon Shrine|ja|龍神社|qq=draft}} – A shrine dedicated to the sea deity, fervently revered by local fishermen who pray for safe voyages and abundant catches. * {{ill|Tashima Myōjin Shrine|ja|淡島明神|qq=draft}} – The Shikinaisha Kada Shrine, enshrined in Kaisō District, Wakayama Prefecture, is known as Tashima Myōjin. It is said that shrines bearing the name Tashima across the country enshrine a divided spirit from this shrine. The offering of combs and hairpins, along with needle memorial services on February 8 and doll memorial services on March 3, are distinctive aspects of this faith. * Kifune {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|qq=draft}} – This shrine consolidates the Inari deities once separately enshrined here and in local households. In recent years, a divided spirit has been enshrined from Fushimi Inari Taisha in Kyoto. A Hatsu-uma ritual is held in conjunction with Setsubun on February 3 each year.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Kifune Festival (Main Festival) == [[File:貴船まつり小早船.jpg|alt=Kifune Festival Kobayabune|Thumbnail|Kifune Festival Kobayabune]] [[File:貴船神社の神輿（貴船まつり）.jpg|alt=Kifune Shrine's Mikoshi (Kifune Festival)|Thumbnail|Kifune Shrine's Mikoshi (Kifune Festival)]] {{詳細記事|貴船まつり}} Kifune Festival is the main festival of {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社|qq=draft}}, a boat festival in which beautifully adorned small swift boats ({{ill|Kobayabune|ja|小早船|qq=draft}}) and mikoshi boats—decorated with elaborate floral arrangements and {{ill|fukinagashi|ja|吹き流し|qq=draft}}—are drawn by {{ill|oar-propelled boats|ja|櫂伝馬船|lt=Oar-propelled boat|lt_ja=櫂伝馬|qq=draft}} in a maritime {{ill|procession|ja|渡御|qq=draft}}. It is held every year on the last {{ill|Saturday|ja|土曜日|qq=draft}} of July and the day before, staged at Manazuru Port and various locations within {{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Ceremonies == * {{ill|New Year Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft}} – January 1 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|qq=draft}} – Setsubun in February * {{ill|Prayer for a Good Harvest Festival|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft}} – February 17 * {{ill|Showa Day|ja|昭和の日|lt=Showa Festival|lt_ja=昭和祭|qq=draft}} – April 29 * Summer Purification (Nagoshi no Ōharae) – June 30 * {{ill|Main Festival|ja|例大祭|qq=draft}} ("{{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}}") – Last Saturday of July and the day before * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft}} – November 23 * {{ill|New Year's Eve Ceremony|ja|除夜祭|qq=draft}} – December 31 * Monthly Festival – On the 1st and 15th of every month{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == Access == * Alight at {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tōkaidō Line|ja|東海道線|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Manazuru Station|ja|真鶴駅|qq=draft}}. A 15‐minute walk. * {{ill|Izu Hakone Bus|ja|伊豆箱根バス|qq=draft}}: Alight at the “Miyamae” bus stop. {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft}}{{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡|qq=draft}}{{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町|qq=draft}}真鶴1117 | coordinates = {{ウィキ座標2段度分秒|35|08|59|N|139|08|46|E|}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神|qq=draft}}
{{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神|qq=draft}} | founded = {{ill|Kampyō|ja|寛平|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|qq=draft}} 1 (889){{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社}} | shrine_Rank = Former Local Shrine | annual_festival = {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} (the last Saturday of July and the day before) }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町|qq=draft}}. It enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神|qq=draft}} (おおくにぬしのおおかみ), {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神|qq=draft}} (ことしろぬしのおおかみ), and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神|qq=draft}} (すくなひこなのおおかみ).{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} The main festival, {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} (きぶねまつり), has been designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/matsuri-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} It is not an enshrinement transferred from the {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社|qq=draft}} (きふねじんじゃ) of {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft}}. == History == * {{ill|Kampyō|ja|寛平|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|qq=draft}} 1 ({{ill|889|ja|889|qq=draft}}) – It is said that Hiraio, the ancestral figure of the {{ill|Shrine family|ja|社家|1=Shake (social class)|2=ja|3=社家|4=id|5=Shake (kelas sosial)|comment=Is there an actual longstanding pun comparing this to the Shakya clan of Siddartha or is that just chatGPT being wonky|lt=Shrine family|qq=draft}} Hirai family ({{ill|Ancestor|ja|祖先|qq=draft}}), discovered twelve wooden statues and a {{ill|letter|ja|書状|qq=draft}}—which had appeared offshore near Mitsuishi ({{ill|Manazuru Cape|ja|真鶴岬|qq=draft}})—and built a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft}}, establishing it as the {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|鎮守神|lt=鎮守|lt_ja=鎮守|qq=draft}} of the village and naming it Kiminomiya (貴宮) {{ill|Great Deity|ja|大明神|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/kibune-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft}} 12 ({{ill|1678|ja|1678|qq=draft}}) – According to “Sōshū West Part, Westward Line, Manazuru Village Shogakuchō” (Documents of the Gomi Family), it is recorded that as early as then, a shipboard {{ill|prayer ritual|ja|祈祷|qq=draft}}—which may be regarded as the origin of the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}}—was performed at Manazuru Port.{{Cite web |title=海上渡御 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/kaijoutogyo.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}} 1 ({{ill|1868|ja|1868|qq=draft}}) – Renamed to '''Kifune Shrine'''.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}} 6 ({{ill|1873|ja|1873|qq=draft}}) {{ill|July|ja|7月|qq=draft}} – Designated as a local shrine ({{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft}}).{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}} 24 ({{ill|1891|ja|1891|qq=draft}}) – Designated as the {{ill|Sacred Provisions Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Sacred Provisions Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft}} 12 ({{ill|1923|ja|1923|qq=draft}}) {{ill|September|ja|9月|qq=draft}} – The shrine building was destroyed by the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft}} 33 ({{ill|1958|ja|1958|qq=draft}}) Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft}} 51 ({{ill|1976|ja|1976|qq=draft}}) {{ill|October 19|ja|10月19日|qq=draft}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft}} 59 ({{ill|1984|ja|1984|qq=draft}}) {{ill|June|ja|6月|qq=draft}} – Prior to the 1,100‐year cycle relocation ceremony, a {{ill|gemstone wall|ja|玉垣|qq=draft}} constructed from Manazuru‐produced {{ill|Komatsu Stone|ja|本小松石|qq=draft}} was completed.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft}} 4 ({{ill|1992|ja|1992|qq=draft}}) {{ill|July|ja|7月|qq=draft}} – 48 kasuga {{ill|lanterns|ja|灯籠|lt=lanterns|lt_ja=灯籠|qq=draft}} made of {{ill|granite|ja|御影石|qq=draft}} were erected on both sides of the grand staircase.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft}} 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996|qq=draft}}) {{ill|December 20|ja|12月20日|qq=draft}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft}} 17 ({{ill|2005|ja|2005|qq=draft}}) {{ill|November 20|ja|11月20日|qq=draft}} – The Nishikobayabune, Kiminomiya Maru, and Kashima Dance from the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} participated in the maritime parade of the National Sea Enrichment Festival held in the {{ill|Yokohama Minato Mirai District|ja|横浜みなとみらい地区|qq=draft}}, which was attended by the {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=小早船 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/kobayabune.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft}}}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神|qq=draft}}{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == Shrine Grounds == * [[File:Honden, Kibube-jinja, Manazuru 2018-01-01.jpg|Thumbnail|Main Hall]] {{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿|qq=draft}} – The main hall of {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社|qq=draft}} was relocated onto the current 108‐step purifying {{ill|stone steps|ja|石段|qq=draft}} after the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft}}, in order to avoid {{ill|flood damage|ja|水害|qq=draft}}. It is a rare shrine building nationwide, constructed with {{ill|reinforced concrete|ja|鉄筋コンクリート|lt=Reinforced|lt_ja=鉄筋|qq=draft}}, which was regarded at the time as the most earthquake‐resistant. Inside the main hall, carvings—such as the sliding doors from the old shrine building—are enshrined. * Wish-Granting Stone – A stone enshrined in the inner garden of the {{ill|Evil-Avoidance Purification Gate|ja|厄除厄祓門|qq=draft}}, offered by devotees in prayer for the fulfillment of their wishes. * Evil-Avoidance and Evil-Purging Gate – A stone gate modeled after the {{ill|doll|ja|人形|lt=Doll|lt_ja=ひとがた|qq=draft}} used in the Shinto purification ritual known as {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓|qq=draft}}, offered by philanthropists in hopes of ensuring community prosperity. * Shrine Boat – A model of the maritime procession—the highlight of the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}}—is on display. The wall depicts the scene on the festival’s first day when the sacred spirit is transferred from the port onto the boat and then proceeds toward the “Sacred Travel Quarters” (御旅所). * Mikoshi Hall – It houses two {{ill|mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft}} used in the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}}. * Purifying Stone Steps – The 108 stone steps leading to the main hall are called the “Purifying Stone Steps” because their number equals that of {{ill|worldly desires|ja|煩悩|qq=draft}}, and they are regarded as a path of {{ill|spiritual training|ja|修行|qq=draft}}. They are made from the finest “blue” {{ill|Komatsu Stone|ja|小松石|qq=draft}} produced locally, their appearance becoming especially striking when wet. * {{ill|Shrine Office|ja|社務所|qq=draft}} * Yoritomo's Seat Stone – According to a famous legend, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft}}, defeated in the {{ill|Ishibashiyama Campaign|ja|石橋山合戦|qq=draft}} (1180), hid in the “Shitodo Cave” and set sail from the {{ill|rocky coast|ja|岩海水浴場|lt=Rocky Coast|lt_ja=岩海岸|qq=draft}}. The rock upon which he is said to have rested during his retreat is enshrined on these grounds as “Yoritomo's Seat Stone.” * {{ill|Memorial Monument|ja|慰霊碑|qq=draft}} – During the Pacific War, many locals lost their lives. Every August 15, a ceremony to commemorate the war dead and pray for peace is held before the monument, attended by bereaved families and numerous others.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} === Shrine Subsidiaries === * {{ill|Ancestral Spirit Shrine|ja|祖霊社|qq=draft}} – A shrine dedicated to the town’s principal parishioners and war dead; when the new main hall was built, the old shrine building was retained as this subsidiary shrine. * {{ill|Mountain Shrine|ja|山神社|qq=draft}} – Although it had been enshrined since ancient times within the “Ohayashi (Fish-Attached Preserved Forest)” of the Manazuru Peninsula, its remote location made worship inconvenient; thus, it was re-enshrined within these grounds in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}} 1 (1868). * {{ill|Dragon Shrine|ja|龍神社|qq=draft}} – A shrine dedicated to the sea deity, fervently revered by local fishermen who pray for safe voyages and abundant catches. * {{ill|Tashima Myōjin Shrine|ja|淡島明神|qq=draft}} – The Shikinaisha Kada Shrine, enshrined in Kaisō District, Wakayama Prefecture, is known as Tashima Myōjin. It is said that shrines bearing the name Tashima across the country enshrine a divided spirit from this shrine. The offering of combs and hairpins, along with needle memorial services on February 8 and doll memorial services on March 3, are distinctive aspects of this faith. * Kifune {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|qq=draft}} – This shrine consolidates the Inari deities once separately enshrined here and in local households. In recent years, a divided spirit has been enshrined from Fushimi Inari Taisha in Kyoto. A Hatsu-uma ritual is held in conjunction with Setsubun on February 3 each year.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Kifune Festival (Main Festival) == [[File:貴船まつり小早船.jpg|alt=Kifune Festival Kobayabune|Thumbnail|Kifune Festival Kobayabune]] [[File:貴船神社の神輿（貴船まつり）.jpg|alt=Kifune Shrine's Mikoshi (Kifune Festival)|Thumbnail|Kifune Shrine's Mikoshi (Kifune Festival)]] {{詳細記事|貴船まつり}} Kifune Festival is the main festival of {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社|qq=draft}}, a boat festival in which beautifully adorned small swift boats ({{ill|Kobayabune|ja|小早船|qq=draft}}) and mikoshi boats—decorated with elaborate floral arrangements and {{ill|fukinagashi|ja|吹き流し|qq=draft}}—are drawn by {{ill|oar-propelled boats|ja|櫂伝馬船|lt=Oar-propelled boat|lt_ja=櫂伝馬|qq=draft}} in a maritime {{ill|procession|ja|渡御|qq=draft}}. It is held every year on the last {{ill|Saturday|ja|土曜日|qq=draft}} of July and the day before, staged at Manazuru Port and various locations within {{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Ceremonies == * {{ill|New Year Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft}} – January 1 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|qq=draft}} – Setsubun in February * {{ill|Prayer for a Good Harvest Festival|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft}} – February 17 * {{ill|Showa Day|ja|昭和の日|lt=Showa Festival|lt_ja=昭和祭|qq=draft}} – April 29 * Summer Purification (Nagoshi no Ōharae) – June 30 * {{ill|Main Festival|ja|例大祭|qq=draft}} ("{{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}}") – Last Saturday of July and the day before * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft}} – November 23 * {{ill|New Year's Eve Ceremony|ja|除夜祭|qq=draft}} – December 31 * Monthly Festival – On the 1st and 15th of every month{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == Access == * Alight at {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tōkaidō Line|ja|東海道線|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Manazuru Station|ja|真鶴駅|qq=draft}}. A 15‐minute walk. * {{ill|Izu Hakone Bus|ja|伊豆箱根バス|qq=draft}}: Alight at the “Miyamae” bus stop. (Walk 0 minutes.) == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|和書|title=The Great Series of Japanese Geography|volume=Volume 37, New Compilation of Sagami Fudoki, 2nd Volume, Section {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神|qq=draft}} | founded = {{ill|Kampyō|ja|寛平|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|qq=draft}} 1 (889){{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社}} | shrine_Rank = Former Local Shrine | annual_festival = {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} (the last Saturday of July and the day before) }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|ja|神社|qq=draft}} located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District|ja|足柄下郡|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町|qq=draft}}. It enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神|qq=draft}} (おおくにぬしのおおかみ), {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神|qq=draft}} (ことしろぬしのおおかみ), and {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神|qq=draft}} (すくなひこなのおおかみ).{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} The main festival, {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} (きぶねまつり), has been designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/matsuri-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} It is not an enshrinement transferred from the {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社|qq=draft}} (きふねじんじゃ) of {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|ja|京都府|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Kyoto City|ja|京都市|qq=draft}}. == History == * {{ill|Kampyō|ja|寛平|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|1=Kanpyō (era)|lt=Kanpyō|qq= (era)|qq=draft}} 1 ({{ill|889|ja|889|qq=draft}}) – It is said that Hiraio, the ancestral figure of the {{ill|Shrine family|ja|社家|1=Shake (social class)|2=ja|3=社家|4=id|5=Shake (kelas sosial)|comment=Is there an actual longstanding pun comparing this to the Shakya clan of Siddartha or is that just chatGPT being wonky|lt=Shrine family|qq=draft}} Hirai family ({{ill|Ancestor|ja|祖先|qq=draft}}), discovered twelve wooden statues and a {{ill|letter|ja|書状|qq=draft}}—which had appeared offshore near Mitsuishi ({{ill|Manazuru Cape|ja|真鶴岬|qq=draft}})—and built a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|qq=draft}}, establishing it as the {{ill|Guardian Deity|ja|鎮守神|lt=鎮守|lt_ja=鎮守|qq=draft}} of the village and naming it Kiminomiya (貴宮) {{ill|Great Deity|ja|大明神|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/kibune-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Kanbun|ja|寛文|qq=draft}} 12 ({{ill|1678|ja|1678|qq=draft}}) – According to “Sōshū West Part, Westward Line, Manazuru Village Shogakuchō” (Documents of the Gomi Family), it is recorded that as early as then, a shipboard {{ill|prayer ritual|ja|祈祷|qq=draft}}—which may be regarded as the origin of the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}}—was performed at Manazuru Port.{{Cite web |title=海上渡御 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/kaijoutogyo.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}} 1 ({{ill|1868|ja|1868|qq=draft}}) – Renamed to '''Kifune Shrine'''.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}} 6 ({{ill|1873|ja|1873|qq=draft}}) {{ill|July|ja|7月|qq=draft}} – Designated as a local shrine ({{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|qq=draft}}).{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}} 24 ({{ill|1891|ja|1891|qq=draft}}) – Designated as the {{ill|Sacred Provisions Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Sacred Provisions Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進神社|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Taishō|ja|大正|qq=draft}} 12 ({{ill|1923|ja|1923|qq=draft}}) {{ill|September|ja|9月|qq=draft}} – The shrine building was destroyed by the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft}} 33 ({{ill|1958|ja|1958|qq=draft}}) {{ill|November 28|ja|11月28日|qq=draft}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Cultural Property|ja|無形文化財|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft}} 51 ({{ill|1976|ja|1976|qq=draft}}) {{ill|October 19|ja|10月19日|qq=draft}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|qq=draft}} by {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|ja|神奈川県|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Shōwa|ja|昭和|qq=draft}} 59 ({{ill|1984|ja|1984|qq=draft}}) {{ill|June|ja|6月|qq=draft}} – Prior to the 1,100‐year cycle relocation ceremony, a {{ill|gemstone wall|ja|玉垣|qq=draft}} constructed from Manazuru‐produced {{ill|Komatsu Stone|ja|本小松石|qq=draft}} was completed.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft}} 4 ({{ill|1992|ja|1992|qq=draft}}) {{ill|July|ja|7月|qq=draft}} – 48 kasuga {{ill|lanterns|ja|灯籠|lt=lanterns|lt_ja=灯籠|qq=draft}} made of {{ill|granite|ja|御影石|qq=draft}} were erected on both sides of the grand staircase.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft}} 8 ({{ill|1996|ja|1996|qq=draft}}) {{ill|December 20|ja|12月20日|qq=draft}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Heisei|ja|平成|qq=draft}} 17 ({{ill|2005|ja|2005|qq=draft}}) {{ill|November 20|ja|11月20日|qq=draft}} – The Nishikobayabune, Kiminomiya Maru, and Kashima Dance from the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}} participated in the maritime parade of the National Sea Enrichment Festival held in the {{ill|Yokohama Minato Mirai District|ja|横浜みなとみらい地区|qq=draft}}, which was attended by the {{ill|Emperor|ja|天皇|qq=draft}} and {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=小早船 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/kobayabune.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|qq=draft}}}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神|qq=draft}}{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == Shrine Grounds == * [[File:Honden, Kibube-jinja, Manazuru 2018-01-01.jpg|Thumbnail|Main Hall]] {{ill|Main Hall|ja|本殿|qq=draft}} – The main hall of {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社|qq=draft}} was relocated onto the current 108‐step purifying {{ill|stone steps|ja|石段|qq=draft}} after the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|qq=draft}}, in order to avoid {{ill|flood damage|ja|水害|qq=draft}}. It is a rare shrine building nationwide, constructed with {{ill|reinforced concrete|ja|鉄筋コンクリート|lt=Reinforced|lt_ja=鉄筋|qq=draft}}, which was regarded at the time as the most earthquake‐resistant. Inside the main hall, carvings—such as the sliding doors from the old shrine building—are enshrined. * Wish-Granting Stone – A stone enshrined in the inner garden of the {{ill|Evil-Avoidance Purification Gate|ja|厄除厄祓門|qq=draft}}, offered by devotees in prayer for the fulfillment of their wishes. * Evil-Avoidance and Evil-Purging Gate – A stone gate modeled after the {{ill|doll|ja|人形|lt=Doll|lt_ja=ひとがた|qq=draft}} used in the Shinto purification ritual known as {{ill|Ōharae|ja|大祓|qq=draft}}, offered by philanthropists in hopes of ensuring community prosperity. * Shrine Boat – A model of the maritime procession—the highlight of the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}}—is on display. The wall depicts the scene on the festival’s first day when the sacred spirit is transferred from the port onto the boat and then proceeds toward the “Sacred Travel Quarters” (御旅所). * Mikoshi Hall – It houses two {{ill|mikoshi|ja|神輿|qq=draft}} used in the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}}. * Purifying Stone Steps – The 108 stone steps leading to the main hall are called the “Purifying Stone Steps” because their number equals that of {{ill|worldly desires|ja|煩悩|qq=draft}}, and they are regarded as a path of {{ill|spiritual training|ja|修行|qq=draft}}. They are made from the finest “blue” {{ill|Komatsu Stone|ja|小松石|qq=draft}} produced locally, their appearance becoming especially striking when wet. * {{ill|Shrine Office|ja|社務所|qq=draft}} * Yoritomo's Seat Stone – According to a famous legend, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|ja|源頼朝|qq=draft}}, defeated in the {{ill|Ishibashiyama Campaign|ja|石橋山合戦|qq=draft}} (1180), hid in the “Shitodo Cave” and set sail from the {{ill|rocky coast|ja|岩海水浴場|lt=Rocky Coast|lt_ja=岩海岸|qq=draft}}. The rock upon which he is said to have rested during his retreat is enshrined on these grounds as “Yoritomo's Seat Stone.” * {{ill|Memorial Monument|ja|慰霊碑|qq=draft}} – During the Pacific War, many locals lost their lives. Every August 15, a ceremony to commemorate the war dead and pray for peace is held before the monument, attended by bereaved families and numerous others.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} === Shrine Subsidiaries === * {{ill|Ancestral Spirit Shrine|ja|祖霊社|qq=draft}} – A shrine dedicated to the town’s principal parishioners and war dead; when the new main hall was built, the old shrine building was retained as this subsidiary shrine. * {{ill|Mountain Shrine|ja|山神社|qq=draft}} – Although it had been enshrined since ancient times within the “Ohayashi (Fish-Attached Preserved Forest)” of the Manazuru Peninsula, its remote location made worship inconvenient; thus, it was re-enshrined within these grounds in {{ill|Meiji|ja|明治|1=Meiji era|qq=draft}} 1 (1868). * {{ill|Dragon Shrine|ja|龍神社|qq=draft}} – A shrine dedicated to the sea deity, fervently revered by local fishermen who pray for safe voyages and abundant catches. * {{ill|Tashima Myōjin Shrine|ja|淡島明神|qq=draft}} – The Shikinaisha Kada Shrine, enshrined in Kaisō District, Wakayama Prefecture, is known as Tashima Myōjin. It is said that shrines bearing the name Tashima across the country enshrine a divided spirit from this shrine. The offering of combs and hairpins, along with needle memorial services on February 8 and doll memorial services on March 3, are distinctive aspects of this faith. * Kifune {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|qq=draft}} – This shrine consolidates the Inari deities once separately enshrined here and in local households. In recent years, a divided spirit has been enshrined from Fushimi Inari Taisha in Kyoto. A Hatsu-uma ritual is held in conjunction with Setsubun on February 3 each year.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Kifune Festival (Main Festival) == [[File:貴船まつり小早船.jpg|alt=Kifune Festival Kobayabune|Thumbnail|Kifune Festival Kobayabune]] [[File:貴船神社の神輿（貴船まつり）.jpg|alt=Kifune Shrine's Mikoshi (Kifune Festival)|Thumbnail|Kifune Shrine's Mikoshi (Kifune Festival)]] {{詳細記事|貴船まつり}} Kifune Festival is the main festival of {{ill|Kifune Shrine|ja|貴船神社|qq=draft}}, a boat festival in which beautifully adorned small swift boats ({{ill|Kobayabune|ja|小早船|qq=draft}}) and mikoshi boats—decorated with elaborate floral arrangements and {{ill|fukinagashi|ja|吹き流し|qq=draft}}—are drawn by {{ill|oar-propelled boats|ja|櫂伝馬船|lt=Oar-propelled boat|lt_ja=櫂伝馬|qq=draft}} in a maritime {{ill|procession|ja|渡御|qq=draft}}. It is held every year on the last {{ill|Saturday|ja|土曜日|qq=draft}} of July and the day before, staged at Manazuru Port and various locations within {{ill|Manazuru|ja|真鶴町|qq=draft}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Ceremonies == * {{ill|New Year Festival|ja|歳旦祭|qq=draft}} – January 1 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|qq=draft}} – Setsubun in February * {{ill|Prayer for a Good Harvest Festival|ja|祈年祭|qq=draft}} – February 17 * {{ill|Showa Day|ja|昭和の日|lt=Showa Festival|lt_ja=昭和祭|qq=draft}} – April 29 * Summer Purification (Nagoshi no Ōharae) – June 30 * {{ill|Main Festival|ja|例大祭|qq=draft}} ("{{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|qq=draft}}") – Last Saturday of July and the day before * {{ill|Niinamesai|ja|新嘗祭|qq=draft}} – November 23 * {{ill|New Year's Eve Ceremony|ja|除夜祭|qq=draft}} – December 31 * Monthly Festival – On the 1st and 15th of every month{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == Access == * Alight at {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|qq=draft}}, {{ill|Tōkaidō Line|ja|東海道線|qq=draft}}, and {{ill|Manazuru Station|ja|真鶴駅|qq=draft}}. A 15‐minute walk. * {{ill|Izu Hakone Bus|ja|伊豆箱根バス|qq=draft}}: Alight at the “Miyamae” bus stop. (Walk 0 minutes.) == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|和書|title=The Great Series of Japanese Geography|volume=Volume 37, New Compilation of Sagami Fudoki, 2nd Volume, Section 32, Ashigarashimo District, Volume 11|chapter=Hayakawashō, Manazuru Village, Kiminomiya Myōjin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179210/86}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社}}|publisher=Yūzankaku|date=1932-8}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/documents/29332/mokuroku.pdf|title=Kanagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Inventory|website=Kanagawa Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-6|date=2021-5-1|ref={{sfnref|神奈川県ホームページ}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/674|title=Boat Festival of Kifune Shrine|website=National Designated Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-6|ref={{sfnref|国指定文化財等データベース}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kiminomiya Faith|ja|キノミヤ信仰|qq=draft}} * {{ill|Kashima Dance|ja|鹿島踊り|qq=draft}} == External Links == * [http://kibunejinja.com/ Kifune Shrine] * [https://kibunematsuri.jp/ Manazuru Kifune Festival] ([https://kibunematsuri.jp/ Kifune Festival Promotion Headquarters]) * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/674 National Designated Cultural Properties Database (Agency for Cultural Affairs)] * [http://www.town.manazuru.kanagawa.jp/ Manazuru Town] * [https://www.manazuru.net/ Manazuru Navi (General Incorporated Association Manazuru Town Tourism Association)] {{translated page|ja|貴船神社 (真鶴町)|version=104285000|comment=Imported full ja history}} {{draft categories| [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Former Local Shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Buildings in Manazuru]] [[Category:History of Manazuru]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:Intangible Folk Cultural Properties Requiring Documentation]] [[Category:Important Intangible Folk Cultural Properties]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] }} [[ja:貴船神社 (真鶴町)]] [[sn:Kifune Shrine (真鶴町)]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]] [[Category:Orphaned pages as of May 10, 2025]]
