[[co:commons:Category:Kibune-jinja (Manazuru, Kanagawa)]][[ja:貴船神社 (真鶴町)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kibune-jinja (Manazuru, Kanagawa)]][[ja:貴船神社 (真鶴町)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kibune-jinja (Manazuru, Kanagawa)]][[ja:貴船神社 (真鶴町)]] {{Otheruseslist|{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|en|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|WD=Q1193172|lt=Ashigarashimo District|1=Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa}}{{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|en|Manazuru, Kanagawa|WD=Q1202786|lt=Manazuru|1=Manazuru, Kanagawa}}にある'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社)|Other Kifune Shrines|Kifune Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Kifune Shrine (Manazuru) | native_name = 貴船神社 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Kinomiya faith|WD=Q11296898|1=Kinomiya Faith}} | image = File:Kibune jinja manaduru kaidan.JPG | address = {{JPN}}
〒259-0201
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|en|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|WD=Q1193172|lt=Ashigarashimo District|1=Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa}}{{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|en|Manazuru, Kanagawa|WD=Q1202786|lt=Manazuru|1=Manazuru, Kanagawa}}真鶴1117 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.149722|N|139.146111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | founded = {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|en|Kanpyō (era)|WD=Q1346292|lt=Kanpyō|1=Kanpyō (era)}} 1 (889){{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社}} | shrine_Rank = Former Local Shrine | annual_festival = {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105338690}} (the last Saturday of July and the day before) | location = {{ill|Manazuru|WD=Q1202786|1=Manazuru, Kanagawa}} | deity = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|en|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|WD=Q1193172|lt=Ashigarashimo District|1=Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa}}, and {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|en|Manazuru, Kanagawa|WD=Q1202786|lt=Manazuru|1=Manazuru, Kanagawa}}. It enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (おおくにぬしのおおかみ), {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (ことしろぬしのおおかみ), and {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (すくなひこなのおおかみ).{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} The main festival, {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105338690}} (きぶねまつり), has been designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}}.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/matsuri-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} It is not an enshrinement transferred from the {{ill|Kifune Shrine|en|Kifune Shrine|WD=Q276779|lt=Kifune Shrine|1=Kifune Shrine}} (きふねじんじゃ) of {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} and {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}. == History == * {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|en|Kanpyō (era)|WD=Q1346292|lt=Kanpyō|1=Kanpyō (era)}} 1 ({{ill|889|en|889|WD=Q23833|lt=889|1=889}}) – It is said that Hiraio, the ancestral figure of the {{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|WD=Q11588367|lt=Shrine family|1=Shake (social class)}} Hirai family ({{ill|Ancestor|ja|祖先|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancestor|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 先祖|1=Ancestor|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q402152}}), discovered twelve wooden statues and a {{ill|Letter|en|Letter|WD=Q293618|lt=letter|1=Letter}}—which had appeared offshore near Mitsuishi ({{ill|Manazuru Cape|ja|真鶴岬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manazuru Cape|1=Cape Manazuru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583649}})—and built a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}}, establishing it as the {{ill|Chinjugami|en|Chinjugami|WD=Q97479976|lt=鎮守|1=Chinjugami}} of the village and naming it Kiminomiya (貴宮) {{ill|Great Deity|ja|大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Deity|1=Daimyōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113497901}}.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/kibune-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|WD=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}} 12 ({{ill|1678|en|1678|WD=Q7634|lt=1678|1=1678}}) – According to “Sōshū West Part, Westward Line, Manazuru Village Shogakuchō” (Documents of the Gomi Family), it is recorded that as early as then, a shipboard {{ill|prayer ritual|ja|祈祷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/prayer ritual|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祈り|1=Prayer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q40953}}—which may be regarded as the origin of the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105338690}}—was performed at Manazuru Port.{{Cite web |title=海上渡御 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/kaijoutogyo.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 1 ({{ill|1868|en|1868|WD=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}}) – Renamed to '''Kifune Shrine'''.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|1873|en|1873|WD=Q7757|lt=1873|1=1873}}) {{ill|July|en|July|WD=Q121|lt=July|1=July}} – Designated as a local shrine ({{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|WD=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|WD=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}).{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 24 ({{ill|1891|en|1891|WD=Q7837|lt=1891|1=1891}}) – Designated as the {{ill|Sacred Provisions Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Sacred Provisions Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Provisions Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q712534}}.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|WD=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} 12 ({{ill|1923|en|1923|WD=Q2169|lt=1923|1=1923}}) {{ill|September|en|September|WD=Q123|lt=September|1=September}} – The shrine building was destroyed by the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 33 ({{ill|1958|en|1958|WD=Q5253|lt=1958|1=1958}}) {{ill|November 28|en|November 28|WD=Q3017|lt=November 28|1=November 28}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105338690}} was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|WD=Q2901957|lt=Intangible Cultural Property|1=Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)}} by {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 51 ({{ill|1976|en|1976|WD=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}}) {{ill|October 19|en|October 19|WD=Q2961|lt=October 19|1=October 19}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105338690}} was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24895900}} by {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|WD=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 59 ({{ill|1984|en|1984|WD=Q2432|lt=1984|1=1984}}) {{ill|June|en|June|WD=Q120|lt=June|1=June}} – Prior to the 1,100‐year cycle relocation ceremony, a {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|WD=Q3078312|lt=gemstone wall|1=Sacred fences}} constructed from Manazuru‐produced {{ill|Komatsu Stone|ja|本小松石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komatsu Stone|1=Komatsu Stone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28691134}} was completed.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 4 ({{ill|1992|en|1992|WD=Q2060|lt=1992|1=1992}}) {{ill|July|en|July|WD=Q121|lt=July|1=July}} – 48 kasuga {{ill|Stone lantern|en|Stone lantern|WD=Q12012294|lt=lanterns|1=Stone lantern}} made of {{ill|granite|ja|御影石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/granite|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 花崗岩|1=Granite|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41177}} were erected on both sides of the grand staircase.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 8 ({{ill|1996|en|1996|WD=Q2070|lt=1996|1=1996}}) {{ill|December 20|en|December 20|WD=Q2455|lt=December 20|1=December 20}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105338690}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|WD=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 17 ({{ill|2005|en|2005|WD=Q2019|lt=2005|1=2005}}) {{ill|November 20|en|November 20|WD=Q3024|lt=November 20|1=November 20}} – The Nishikobayabune, Kiminomiya Maru, and Kashima Dance from the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105338690}} participated in the maritime parade of the National Sea Enrichment Festival held in the {{ill|Yokohama Minato Mirai District|ja|横浜みなとみらい地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Minato Mirai District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 横浜みなとみらい21|1=Minato Mirai 21|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1140617}}, which was attended by the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|WD=Q208233|lt=Emperor|1=Emperor of Japan}} and {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Empress consort|WD=Q7723211}}.{{Cite web |title=小早船 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/kobayabune.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == Shrine Grounds == * [[File:Honden, Kibube-jinja, Manazuru 2018-01-01.jpg|Thumbnail|Main Hall]] {{ill|Honden|en|Honden|WD=Q2044994|lt=Main Hall|1=Honden}} – The main hall of {{ill|Kifune Shrine|en|Kifune Shrine|WD=Q276779|lt=Kifune Shrine|1=Kifune Shrine}} was relocated onto the current 108‐step purifying {{ill|stone steps|ja|石段|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone steps|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 階段|1=Stairs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12511}} after the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}, in order to avoid {{ill|flood damage|ja|水害|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flood damage|1=water-related disaster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11252966}}. It is a rare shrine building nationwide, constructed with {{ill|Reinforced concrete|en|Reinforced concrete|WD=Q184190|lt=Reinforced|1=Reinforced concrete}}, which was regarded at the time as the most earthquake‐resistant. Inside the main hall, carvings—such as the sliding doors from the old shrine building—are enshrined. * Wish-Granting Stone – A stone enshrined in the inner garden of the {{ill|Evil-Avoidance Purification Gate|ja|厄除厄祓門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Evil-Avoidance Purification Gate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, offered by devotees in prayer for the fulfillment of their wishes. * Evil-Avoidance and Evil-Purging Gate – A stone gate modeled after the {{ill|Doll|en|Doll|WD=Q168658|lt=Doll|1=Doll}} used in the Shinto purification ritual known as {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|en|Ōharae-shiki|WD=Q11261910|lt=Ōharae|1=Ōharae-shiki}}, offered by philanthropists in hopes of ensuring community prosperity. * Shrine Boat – A model of the maritime procession—the highlight of the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105338690}}—is on display. The wall depicts the scene on the festival’s first day when the sacred spirit is transferred from the port onto the boat and then proceeds toward the “Sacred Travel Quarters” (御旅所). * Mikoshi Hall – It houses two {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}} used in the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105338690}}. * Purifying Stone Steps – The 108 stone steps leading to the main hall are called the “Purifying Stone Steps” because their number equals that of {{ill|Kleshas (Buddhism)|en|Kleshas (Buddhism)|WD=Q19969527|lt=worldly desires|1=Kleshas (Buddhism)}}, and they are regarded as a path of {{ill|Sādhanā|en|Sādhanā|WD=Q507662|lt=spiritual training|1=Sādhanā}}. They are made from the finest “blue” {{ill|Komatsu Stone|ja|小松石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komatsu Stone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本小松石|1=Komatsu Stone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28691134}} produced locally, their appearance becoming especially striking when wet. * {{ill|Shrine Office|ja|社務所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社|1=Shinto shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q845945}} * Yoritomo's Seat Stone – According to a famous legend, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, defeated in the {{ill|Ishibashiyama Campaign|ja|石橋山合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibashiyama Campaign|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石橋山の戦い|1=Battle of Ishibashiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2888010}} (1180), hid in the “Shitodo Cave” and set sail from the {{ill|rocky coast|ja|岩海水浴場|lt=Rocky Coast|lt_ja=岩海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rocky coast|1=Iwa Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38277589}}. The rock upon which he is said to have rested during his retreat is enshrined on these grounds as “Yoritomo's Seat Stone.” * {{ill|Cenotaph|en|Cenotaph|WD=Q321053|lt=Memorial Monument|1=Cenotaph}} – During the Pacific War, many locals lost their lives. Every August 15, a ceremony to commemorate the war dead and pray for peace is held before the monument, attended by bereaved families and numerous others.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} === Shrine Subsidiaries === * {{ill|Ancestral Spirit Shrine|ja|祖霊社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancestral Spirit Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A shrine dedicated to the town’s principal parishioners and war dead; when the new main hall was built, the old shrine building was retained as this subsidiary shrine. * {{ill|Mountain Shrine|ja|山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain Shrine|qq=}} – Although it had been enshrined since ancient times within the “Ohayashi (Fish-Attached Preserved Forest)” of the Manazuru Peninsula, its remote location made worship inconvenient; thus, it was re-enshrined within these grounds in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 1 (1868). * {{ill|Dragon Shrine|ja|龍神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dragon Shrine|1=Ryūjin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679331}} – A shrine dedicated to the sea deity, fervently revered by local fishermen who pray for safe voyages and abundant catches. * {{ill|Tashima Myōjin Shrine|ja|淡島明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashima Myōjin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淡島神|1=Awashima-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1575740}} – The Shikinaisha Kada Shrine, enshrined in Kaisō District, Wakayama Prefecture, is known as Tashima Myōjin. It is said that shrines bearing the name Tashima across the country enshrine a divided spirit from this shrine. The offering of combs and hairpins, along with needle memorial services on February 8 and doll memorial services on March 3, are distinctive aspects of this faith. * Kifune {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q514480}} – This shrine consolidates the Inari deities once separately enshrined here and in local households. In recent years, a divided spirit has been enshrined from Fushimi Inari Taisha in Kyoto. A Hatsu-uma ritual is held in conjunction with Setsubun on February 3 each year.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Kifune Festival (Main Festival) == [[File:貴船まつり小早船.jpg|alt=Kifune Festival Kobayabune|Thumbnail|Kifune Festival Kobayabune]] [[File:貴船神社の神輿（貴船まつり）.jpg|alt=Kifune Shrine's Mikoshi (Kifune Festival)|Thumbnail|Kifune Shrine's Mikoshi (Kifune Festival)]] {{詳細記事|貴船まつり}} Kifune Festival is the main festival of {{ill|Kifune Shrine|en|Kifune Shrine|WD=Q276779|lt=Kifune Shrine|1=Kifune Shrine}}, a boat festival in which beautifully adorned small swift boats ({{ill|Kobayabune|ja|小早船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobayabune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小早|1=kohaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8017865}}) and mikoshi boats—decorated with elaborate floral arrangements and {{ill|fukinagashi|ja|吹き流し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukinagashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吹流し|1=Windsock|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q216346}}—are drawn by {{ill|oar-propelled boats|ja|櫂伝馬船|lt=Oar-propelled boat|lt_ja=櫂伝馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/oar-propelled boats|1=kaidenmasen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544651}} in a maritime {{ill|procession|ja|渡御|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/procession|1=Q11561919|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561919}}. It is held every year on the last {{ill|Saturday|en|Saturday|WD=Q131|lt=Saturday|1=Saturday}} of July and the day before, staged at Manazuru Port and various locations within {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|en|Manazuru, Kanagawa|WD=Q1202786|lt=Manazuru|1=Manazuru, Kanagawa}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Ceremonies == * {{ill|New Year Festival|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Festival|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} – January 1 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q746798}} – Setsubun in February * {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Prayer for a Good Harvest Festival|1=Kinensai}} – February 17 * {{ill|Shōwa Day|en|Shōwa Day|WD=Q1361434|lt=Showa Festival|1=Shōwa Day}} – April 29 * Summer Purification (Nagoshi no Ōharae) – June 30 * {{ill|Main Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Main Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} ("{{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105338690}}") – Last Saturday of July and the day before * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} – November 23 * {{ill|New Year's Eve Ceremony|ja|除夜祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Eve Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – December 31 * Monthly Festival – On the 1st and 15th of every month{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == Access == * Alight at {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}, {{ill|Tōkaidō Line|ja|東海道線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東海道本線|1=Tōkaidō Main Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1190152}}, and {{ill|Manazuru Station|en|Manazuru Station|WD=Q865802|lt=Manazuru Station|1=Manazuru Station}}. A 15‐minute walk. * {{ill|Izu Hakone Bus|ja|伊豆箱根バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Hakone Bus|1=Izuhakone Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11380590}}: Alight at the “Miyamae” bus stop. (Walk 0 minutes.) == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|和書|title=The Great Series of Japanese Geography|volume=Volume 37, New Compilation of Sagami Fudoki, 2nd Volume, Section 32, Ashigarashimo District, Volume 11|chapter=Hayakawashō, Manazuru Village, Kiminomiya Myōjin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179210/86}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社}}|publisher=Yūzankaku|date=1932-8}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/documents/29332/mokuroku.pdf|title=Kanagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Inventory|website=Kanagawa Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-6|date=2021-5-1|ref={{sfnref|神奈川県ホームページ}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/674|title=Boat Festival of Kifune Shrine|website=National Designated Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-6|ref={{sfnref|国指定文化財等データベース}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kiminomiya Faith|ja|キノミヤ信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiminomiya Faith|1=Kinomiya Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11296898}} * {{ill|Kashima Dance|ja|鹿島踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Dance|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}} == External Links == * [http://kibunejinja.com/ Kifune Shrine] * [https://kibunematsuri.jp/ Manazuru Kifune Festival] ([https://kibunematsuri.jp/ Kifune Festival Promotion Headquarters]) * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/674 National Designated Cultural Properties Database (Agency for Cultural Affairs)] * [http://www.town.manazuru.kanagawa.jp/ Manazuru Town] * [https://www.manazuru.net/ Manazuru Navi (General Incorporated Association Manazuru Town Tourism Association)] {{translated {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | founded = {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|en|Kanpyō (era)|WD=Q1346292|lt=Kanpyō|1=Kanpyō (era)}} 1 (889){{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社}} | shrine_Rank = Former Local Shrine | annual_festival = {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105338690}} (the last Saturday of July and the day before) | location = {{ill|Manazuru|WD=Q1202786|1=Manazuru, Kanagawa}} | deity = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|WD=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Sukunabikona|WD=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|WD=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|WD=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|WD=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|en|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|WD=Q1193172|lt=Ashigarashimo District|1=Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa}}, and {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|en|Manazuru, Kanagawa|WD=Q1202786|lt=Manazuru|1=Manazuru, Kanagawa}}. It enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (おおくにぬしのおおかみ), {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (ことしろぬしのおおかみ), and {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (すくなひこなのおおかみ).{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} The main festival, {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105338690}} (きぶねまつり), has been designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6573893}}.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/matsuri-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} It is not an enshrinement transferred from the {{ill|Kifune Shrine|en|Kifune Shrine|WD=Q276779|lt=Kifune Shrine|1=Kifune Shrine}} (きふねじんじゃ) of {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} and {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}. == History == * {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|en|Kanpyō (era)|WD=Q1346292|lt=Kanpyō|1=Kanpyō (era)}} 1 ({{ill|889|en|889|WD=Q23833|lt=889|1=889}}) – It is said that Hiraio, the ancestral figure of the {{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|WD=Q11588367|lt=Shrine family|1=Shake (social class)}} Hirai family ({{ill|Ancestor|ja|祖先|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancestor|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 先祖|1=Ancestor|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q402152}}), discovered twelve wooden statues and a {{ill|Letter|en|Letter|WD=Q293618|lt=letter|1=Letter}}—which had appeared offshore near Mitsuishi ({{ill|Manazuru Cape|ja|真鶴岬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manazuru Cape|1=Cape Manazuru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11583649}})—and built a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3080012}}, establishing it as the {{ill|Chinjugami|en|Chinjugami|WD=Q97479976|lt=鎮守|1=Chinjugami}} of the village and naming it Kiminomiya (貴宮) {{ill|Great Deity|ja|大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Deity|1=Daimyōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q113497901}}.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|WD=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == Shrine Grounds == * [[File:Honden, Kibube-jinja, Manazuru 2018-01-01.jpg|Thumbnail|Main Hall]] {{ill|Honden|en|Honden|WD=Q2044994|lt=Main Hall|1=Honden}} – The main hall of {{ill|Kifune Shrine|en|Kifune Shrine|WD=Q276779|lt=Kifune Shrine|1=Kifune Shrine}} was relocated onto the current 108‐step purifying {{ill|stone steps|ja|石段|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone steps|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 階段|1=Stairs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q12511}} after the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q3742341}}, in order to avoid {{ill|flood damage|ja|水害|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flood damage|1=water-related disaster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11252966}}. It is a rare shrine building nationwide, constructed with {{ill|Reinforced concrete|en|Reinforced concrete|WD=Q184190|lt=Reinforced|1=Reinforced concrete}}, which was regarded at the time as the most earthquake‐resistant. Inside the main hall, carvings—such as the sliding doors from the old shrine building—are enshrined. * Wish-Granting Stone – A stone enshrined in the inner garden of the {{ill|Evil-Avoidance Purification Gate|ja|厄除厄祓門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Evil-Avoidance Purification Gate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, offered by devotees in prayer for the fulfillment of their wishes. * Evil-Avoidance and Evil-Purging Gate – A stone gate modeled after the {{ill|Doll|en|Doll|WD=Q168658|lt=Doll|1=Doll}} used in the Shinto purification ritual known as {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|en|Ōharae-shiki|WD=Q11261910|lt=Ōharae|1=Ōharae-shiki}}, offered by philanthropists in hopes of ensuring community prosperity. * Shrine Boat – A model of the maritime procession—the highlight of the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105338690}}—is on display. The wall depicts the scene on the festival’s first day when the sacred spirit is transferred from the port onto the boat and then proceeds toward the “Sacred Travel Quarters” (御旅所). * Mikoshi Hall – It houses two {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|WD=Q979940|lt=mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}} used in the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105338690}}. * Purifying Stone Steps – The 108 stone steps leading to the main hall are called the “Purifying Stone Steps” because their number equals that of {{ill|Kleshas (Buddhism)|en|Kleshas (Buddhism)|WD=Q19969527|lt=worldly desires|1=Kleshas (Buddhism)}}, and they are regarded as a path of {{ill|Sādhanā|en|Sādhanā|WD=Q507662|lt=spiritual training|1=Sādhanā}}. They are made from the finest “blue” {{ill|Komatsu Stone|ja|小松石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komatsu Stone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本小松石|1=Komatsu Stone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q28691134}} produced locally, their appearance becoming especially striking when wet. * {{ill|Shrine Office|ja|社務所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社|1=Shinto shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q845945}} * Yoritomo's Seat Stone – According to a famous legend, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|WD=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, defeated in the {{ill|Ishibashiyama Campaign|ja|石橋山合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibashiyama Campaign|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石橋山の戦い|1=Battle of Ishibashiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q2888010}} (1180), hid in the “Shitodo Cave” and set sail from the {{ill|rocky coast|ja|岩海水浴場|lt=Rocky Coast|lt_ja=岩海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rocky coast|1=Iwa Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q38277589}}. The rock upon which he is said to have rested during his retreat is enshrined on these grounds as “Yoritomo's Seat Stone.” * {{ill|Cenotaph|en|Cenotaph|WD=Q321053|lt=Memorial Monument|1=Cenotaph}} – During the Pacific War, many locals lost their lives. Every August 15, a ceremony to commemorate the war dead and pray for peace is held before the monument, attended by bereaved families and numerous others.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} === Shrine Subsidiaries === * {{ill|Ancestral Spirit Shrine|ja|祖霊社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancestral Spirit Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A shrine dedicated to the town’s principal parishioners and war dead; when the new main hall was built, the old shrine building was retained as this subsidiary shrine. * {{ill|Mountain Shrine|ja|山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain Shrine|qq=}} – Although it had been enshrined since ancient times within the “Ohayashi (Fish-Attached Preserved Forest)” of the Manazuru Peninsula, its remote location made worship inconvenient; thus, it was re-enshrined within these grounds in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|WD=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 1 (1868). * {{ill|Dragon Shrine|ja|龍神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dragon Shrine|1=Ryūjin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11679331}} – A shrine dedicated to the sea deity, fervently revered by local fishermen who pray for safe voyages and abundant catches. * {{ill|Tashima Myōjin Shrine|ja|淡島明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashima Myōjin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淡島神|1=Awashima-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1575740}} – The Shikinaisha Kada Shrine, enshrined in Kaisō District, Wakayama Prefecture, is known as Tashima Myōjin. It is said that shrines bearing the name Tashima across the country enshrine a divided spirit from this shrine. The offering of combs and hairpins, along with needle memorial services on February 8 and doll memorial services on March 3, are distinctive aspects of this faith. * Kifune {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q514480}} – This shrine consolidates the Inari deities once separately enshrined here and in local households. In recent years, a divided spirit has been enshrined from Fushimi Inari Taisha in Kyoto. A Hatsu-uma ritual is held in conjunction with Setsubun on February 3 each year.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Kifune Festival (Main Festival) == [[File:貴船まつり小早船.jpg|alt=Kifune Festival Kobayabune|Thumbnail|Kifune Festival Kobayabune]] [[File:貴船神社の神輿（貴船まつり）.jpg|alt=Kifune Shrine's Mikoshi (Kifune Festival)|Thumbnail|Kifune Shrine's Mikoshi (Kifune Festival)]] {{詳細記事|貴船まつり}} Kifune Festival is the main festival of {{ill|Kifune Shrine|en|Kifune Shrine|WD=Q276779|lt=Kifune Shrine|1=Kifune Shrine}}, a boat festival in which beautifully adorned small swift boats ({{ill|Kobayabune|ja|小早船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobayabune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小早|1=kohaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q8017865}}) and mikoshi boats—decorated with elaborate floral arrangements and {{ill|fukinagashi|ja|吹き流し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukinagashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吹流し|1=Windsock|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q216346}}—are drawn by {{ill|oar-propelled boats|ja|櫂伝馬船|lt=Oar-propelled boat|lt_ja=櫂伝馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/oar-propelled boats|1=kaidenmasen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11544651}} in a maritime {{ill|procession|ja|渡御|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/procession|1=Q11561919|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11561919}}. It is held every year on the last {{ill|Saturday|en|Saturday|WD=Q131|lt=Saturday|1=Saturday}} of July and the day before, staged at Manazuru Port and various locations within {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|en|Manazuru, Kanagawa|WD=Q1202786|lt=Manazuru|1=Manazuru, Kanagawa}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Ceremonies == * {{ill|New Year Festival|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Festival|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11546824}} – January 1 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q746798}} – Setsubun in February * {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|WD=Q118906710|lt=Prayer for a Good Harvest Festival|1=Kinensai}} – February 17 * {{ill|Shōwa Day|en|Shōwa Day|WD=Q1361434|lt=Showa Festival|1=Shōwa Day}} – April 29 * Summer Purification (Nagoshi no Ōharae) – June 30 * {{ill|Main Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Main Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} ("{{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q105338690}}") – Last Saturday of July and the day before * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|WD=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} – November 23 * {{ill|New Year's Eve Ceremony|ja|除夜祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Eve Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – December 31 * Monthly Festival – On the 1st and 15th of every month{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == Access == * Alight at {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q499071}}, {{ill|Tōkaidō Line|ja|東海道線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東海道本線|1=Tōkaidō Main Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q1190152}}, and {{ill|Manazuru Station|en|Manazuru Station|WD=Q865802|lt=Manazuru Station|1=Manazuru Station}}. A 15‐minute walk. * {{ill|Izu Hakone Bus|ja|伊豆箱根バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Hakone Bus|1=Izuhakone Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11380590}}: Alight at the “Miyamae” bus stop. (Walk 0 minutes.) == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|和書|title=The Great Series of Japanese Geography|volume=Volume 37, New Compilation of Sagami Fudoki, 2nd Volume, Section 32, Ashigarashimo District, Volume 11|chapter=Hayakawashō, Manazuru Village, Kiminomiya Myōjin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179210/86}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社}}|publisher=Yūzankaku|date=1932-8}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/documents/29332/mokuroku.pdf|title=Kanagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Inventory|website=Kanagawa Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-6|date=2021-5-1|ref={{sfnref|神奈川県ホームページ}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/674|title=Boat Festival of Kifune Shrine|website=National Designated Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-6|ref={{sfnref|国指定文化財等データベース}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kiminomiya Faith|ja|キノミヤ信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiminomiya Faith|1=Kinomiya Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11296898}} * {{ill|Kashima Dance|ja|鹿島踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Dance|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11677163}} == External Links == * [http://kibunejinja.com/ Kifune Shrine] * [https://kibunematsuri.jp/ Manazuru Kifune Festival] ([https://kibunematsuri.jp/ Kifune Festival Promotion Headquarters]) * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/674 National Designated Cultural Properties Database (Agency for Cultural Affairs)] * [http://www.town.manazuru.kanagawa.jp/ Manazuru Town] * [https://www.manazuru.net/ Manazuru Navi (General Incorporated Association Manazuru Town Tourism Association)] {{translated page|ja|貴船神社 (真鶴町)|version=104285000|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:貴船神社 (真鶴町)]] {{Commons category|Kibune-jinja (Manazuru, Kanagawa)}} {{wikidata link|Q11634812}} [[Category:Buildings and measures such as documentation of Japan]] [[Category:Kifune Shrines]] [[Category:Municipal History of Manazuru]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Kanagawa Prefecture]] [[Category:Wikidata has short [[co:commons:Category:Kibune-jinja (Manazuru, Kanagawa)]][[ja:貴船神社 (真鶴町)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kibune-jinja (Manazuru, Kanagawa)]][[ja:貴船神社 (真鶴町)]] [[Category:sn_crud|TESTcommons:Category:Kibune-jinja (Manazuru, Kanagawa)]][[ja:貴船神社 (真鶴町)]] {{Otheruseslist|{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|en|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|qid=Q1193172|lt=Ashigarashimo District|1=Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa}}{{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|en|Manazuru, Kanagawa|qid=Q1202786|lt=Manazuru|1=Manazuru, Kanagawa}}にある'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社)|Other Kifune Shrines|Kifune Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Kifune Shrine (Manazuru) | native_name = 貴船神社 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Kinomiya faith|qid=Q11296898|1=Kinomiya Faith}} | image = File:Kibune jinja manaduru kaidan.JPG | address = {{JPN}}
〒259-0201
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|en|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|qid=Q1193172|lt=Ashigarashimo District|1=Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa}}{{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|en|Manazuru, Kanagawa|qid=Q1202786|lt=Manazuru|1=Manazuru, Kanagawa}}真鶴1117 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.149722|N|139.146111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | founded = {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|en|Kanpyō (era)|qid=Q1346292|lt=Kanpyō|1=Kanpyō (era)}} 1 (889){{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社}} | shrine_Rank = Former Local Shrine | annual_festival = {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q105338690}} (the last Saturday of July and the day before) | location = {{ill|Manazuru|qid=Q1202786|1=Manazuru, Kanagawa}} | deity = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|qid=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|en|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|qid=Q1193172|lt=Ashigarashimo District|1=Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa}}, and {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|en|Manazuru, Kanagawa|qid=Q1202786|lt=Manazuru|1=Manazuru, Kanagawa}}. It enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (おおくにぬしのおおかみ), {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (ことしろぬしのおおかみ), and {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (すくなひこなのおおかみ).{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} The main festival, {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q105338690}} (きぶねまつり), has been designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6573893}}.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/matsuri-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} It is not an enshrinement transferred from the {{ill|Kifune Shrine|en|Kifune Shrine|qid=Q276779|lt=Kifune Shrine|1=Kifune Shrine}} (きふねじんじゃ) of {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} and {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}. == History == * {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|en|Kanpyō (era)|qid=Q1346292|lt=Kanpyō|1=Kanpyō (era)}} 1 ({{ill|889|en|889|qid=Q23833|lt=889|1=889}}) – It is said that Hiraio, the ancestral figure of the {{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|qid=Q11588367|lt=Shrine family|1=Shake (social class)}} Hirai family ({{ill|Ancestor|ja|祖先|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancestor|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 先祖|1=Ancestor|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q402152}}), discovered twelve wooden statues and a {{ill|Letter|en|Letter|qid=Q293618|lt=letter|1=Letter}}—which had appeared offshore near Mitsuishi ({{ill|Manazuru Cape|ja|真鶴岬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manazuru Cape|1=Cape Manazuru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11583649}})—and built a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3080012}}, establishing it as the {{ill|Chinjugami|en|Chinjugami|qid=Q97479976|lt=鎮守|1=Chinjugami}} of the village and naming it Kiminomiya (貴宮) {{ill|Great Deity|ja|大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Deity|1=Daimyōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q113497901}}.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/kibune-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|qid=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}} 12 ({{ill|1678|en|1678|qid=Q7634|lt=1678|1=1678}}) – According to “Sōshū West Part, Westward Line, Manazuru Village Shogakuchō” (Documents of the Gomi Family), it is recorded that as early as then, a shipboard {{ill|prayer ritual|ja|祈祷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/prayer ritual|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祈り|1=Prayer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q40953}}—which may be regarded as the origin of the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q105338690}}—was performed at Manazuru Port.{{Cite web |title=海上渡御 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/kaijoutogyo.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 1 ({{ill|1868|en|1868|qid=Q7717|lt=1868|1=1868}}) – Renamed to '''Kifune Shrine'''.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 6 ({{ill|1873|en|1873|qid=Q7757|lt=1873|1=1873}}) {{ill|July|en|July|qid=Q121|lt=July|1=July}} – Designated as a local shrine ({{ill|Local Shrine|ja|郷社|1=Gō-sha|qid=Q134917316}}{{efn|District Shrines|qid=Q90139226|lt=Ken-sha|1=modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Local Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}).{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 24 ({{ill|1891|en|1891|qid=Q7837|lt=1891|1=1891}}) – Designated as the {{ill|Sacred Provisions Shrine|ja|神饌幣帛料供進社|lt=Sacred Provisions Shrine|lt_ja=神饌幣帛料供進神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sacred Provisions Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 近代社格制度|1=Modern system of ranked Shinto shrines|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q712534}}.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Taishō era|en|Taishō era|qid=Q329174|lt=Taishō|1=Taishō era}} 12 ({{ill|1923|en|1923|qid=Q2169|lt=1923|1=1923}}) {{ill|September|en|September|qid=Q123|lt=September|1=September}} – The shrine building was destroyed by the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3742341}}.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 33 ({{ill|1958|en|1958|qid=Q5253|lt=1958|1=1958}}) {{ill|November 28|en|November 28|qid=Q3017|lt=November 28|1=November 28}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q105338690}} was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|en|Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)|qid=Q2901957|lt=Intangible Cultural Property|1=Intangible Cultural Property (Japan)}} by {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 51 ({{ill|1976|en|1976|qid=Q2480|lt=1976|1=1976}}) {{ill|October 19|en|October 19|qid=Q2961|lt=October 19|1=October 19}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q105338690}} was designated as an {{ill|Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 民俗文化財|1=Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q24895900}} by {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Shōwa era|en|Shōwa era|qid=Q327567|lt=Shōwa|1=Shōwa era}} 59 ({{ill|1984|en|1984|qid=Q2432|lt=1984|1=1984}}) {{ill|June|en|June|qid=Q120|lt=June|1=June}} – Prior to the 1,100‐year cycle relocation ceremony, a {{ill|Tamagaki|en|Tamagaki|qid=Q3078312|lt=gemstone wall|1=Sacred fences}} constructed from Manazuru‐produced {{ill|Komatsu Stone|ja|本小松石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komatsu Stone|1=Komatsu Stone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28691134}} was completed.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 4 ({{ill|1992|en|1992|qid=Q2060|lt=1992|1=1992}}) {{ill|July|en|July|qid=Q121|lt=July|1=July}} – 48 kasuga {{ill|Stone lantern|en|Stone lantern|qid=Q12012294|lt=lanterns|1=Stone lantern}} made of {{ill|granite|ja|御影石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/granite|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 花崗岩|1=Granite|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q41177}} were erected on both sides of the grand staircase.{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} * {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 8 ({{ill|1996|en|1996|qid=Q2070|lt=1996|1=1996}}) {{ill|December 20|en|December 20|qid=Q2455|lt=December 20|1=December 20}} – The {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q105338690}} was designated as an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6573893}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Heisei era|en|Heisei era|qid=Q234510|lt=Heisei|1=Heisei era}} 17 ({{ill|2005|en|2005|qid=Q2019|lt=2005|1=2005}}) {{ill|November 20|en|November 20|qid=Q3024|lt=November 20|1=November 20}} – The Nishikobayabune, Kiminomiya Maru, and Kashima Dance from the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q105338690}} participated in the maritime parade of the National Sea Enrichment Festival held in the {{ill|Yokohama Minato Mirai District|ja|横浜みなとみらい地区|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yokohama Minato Mirai District|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 横浜みなとみらい21|1=Minato Mirai 21|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1140617}}, which was attended by the {{ill|Emperor of Japan|en|Emperor of Japan|qid=Q208233|lt=Emperor|1=Emperor of Japan}} and {{ill|Empress|ja|皇后|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Empress|comment=changed title based on wikidata|1=Empress consort|qid=Q7723211}}.{{Cite web |title=小早船 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/kobayabune.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Worshipped deities (Saijin) == {{see also|{{ill|Saijin|ja|祭神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Saijin|qq=}}}} * {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} * {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} * {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}}{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == Shrine Grounds == * [[File:Honden, Kibube-jinja, Manazuru 2018-01-01.jpg|Thumbnail|Main Hall]] {{ill|Honden|en|Honden|qid=Q2044994|lt=Main Hall|1=Honden}} – The main hall of {{ill|Kifune Shrine|en|Kifune Shrine|qid=Q276779|lt=Kifune Shrine|1=Kifune Shrine}} was relocated onto the current 108‐step purifying {{ill|stone steps|ja|石段|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/stone steps|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 階段|1=Stairs|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q12511}} after the {{ill|Great Kantō Earthquake|ja|関東大震災|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Kantō Earthquake|1=aftermath of 1923 Great Kantō earthquake|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3742341}}, in order to avoid {{ill|flood damage|ja|水害|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/flood damage|1=water-related disaster|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11252966}}. It is a rare shrine building nationwide, constructed with {{ill|Reinforced concrete|en|Reinforced concrete|qid=Q184190|lt=Reinforced|1=Reinforced concrete}}, which was regarded at the time as the most earthquake‐resistant. Inside the main hall, carvings—such as the sliding doors from the old shrine building—are enshrined. * Wish-Granting Stone – A stone enshrined in the inner garden of the {{ill|Evil-Avoidance Purification Gate|ja|厄除厄祓門|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Evil-Avoidance Purification Gate|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, offered by devotees in prayer for the fulfillment of their wishes. * Evil-Avoidance and Evil-Purging Gate – A stone gate modeled after the {{ill|Doll|en|Doll|qid=Q168658|lt=Doll|1=Doll}} used in the Shinto purification ritual known as {{ill|Ōharae-shiki|en|Ōharae-shiki|qid=Q11261910|lt=Ōharae|1=Ōharae-shiki}}, offered by philanthropists in hopes of ensuring community prosperity. * Shrine Boat – A model of the maritime procession—the highlight of the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q105338690}}—is on display. The wall depicts the scene on the festival’s first day when the sacred spirit is transferred from the port onto the boat and then proceeds toward the “Sacred Travel Quarters” (御旅所). * Mikoshi Hall – It houses two {{ill|Mikoshi|en|Mikoshi|qid=Q979940|lt=mikoshi|1=Mikoshi}} used in the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q105338690}}. * Purifying Stone Steps – The 108 stone steps leading to the main hall are called the “Purifying Stone Steps” because their number equals that of {{ill|Kleshas (Buddhism)|en|Kleshas (Buddhism)|qid=Q19969527|lt=worldly desires|1=Kleshas (Buddhism)}}, and they are regarded as a path of {{ill|Sādhanā|en|Sādhanā|qid=Q507662|lt=spiritual training|1=Sādhanā}}. They are made from the finest “blue” {{ill|Komatsu Stone|ja|小松石|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Komatsu Stone|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 本小松石|1=Komatsu Stone|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q28691134}} produced locally, their appearance becoming especially striking when wet. * {{ill|Shrine Office|ja|社務所|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shrine Office|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社|1=Shinto shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q845945}} * Yoritomo's Seat Stone – According to a famous legend, {{ill|Minamoto no Yoritomo|en|Minamoto no Yoritomo|qid=Q242800|lt=Minamoto no Yoritomo|1=Minamoto no Yoritomo}}, defeated in the {{ill|Ishibashiyama Campaign|ja|石橋山合戦|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishibashiyama Campaign|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 石橋山の戦い|1=Battle of Ishibashiyama|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q2888010}} (1180), hid in the “Shitodo Cave” and set sail from the {{ill|rocky coast|ja|岩海水浴場|lt=Rocky Coast|lt_ja=岩海岸|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/rocky coast|1=Iwa Beach|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q38277589}}. The rock upon which he is said to have rested during his retreat is enshrined on these grounds as “Yoritomo's Seat Stone.” * {{ill|Cenotaph|en|Cenotaph|qid=Q321053|lt=Memorial Monument|1=Cenotaph}} – During the Pacific War, many locals lost their lives. Every August 15, a ceremony to commemorate the war dead and pray for peace is held before the monument, attended by bereaved families and numerous others.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} === Shrine Subsidiaries === * {{ill|Ancestral Spirit Shrine|ja|祖霊社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancestral Spirit Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – A shrine dedicated to the town’s principal parishioners and war dead; when the new main hall was built, the old shrine building was retained as this subsidiary shrine. * {{ill|Mountain Shrine|ja|山神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mountain Shrine|qq=}} – Although it had been enshrined since ancient times within the “Ohayashi (Fish-Attached Preserved Forest)” of the Manazuru Peninsula, its remote location made worship inconvenient; thus, it was re-enshrined within these grounds in {{ill|Meiji era|en|Meiji era|qid=Q3125096|lt=Meiji era|1=Meiji era}} 1 (1868). * {{ill|Dragon Shrine|ja|龍神社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Dragon Shrine|1=Ryūjin Shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11679331}} – A shrine dedicated to the sea deity, fervently revered by local fishermen who pray for safe voyages and abundant catches. * {{ill|Tashima Myōjin Shrine|ja|淡島明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tashima Myōjin Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 淡島神|1=Awashima-no-Kami|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1575740}} – The Shikinaisha Kada Shrine, enshrined in Kaisō District, Wakayama Prefecture, is known as Tashima Myōjin. It is said that shrines bearing the name Tashima across the country enshrine a divided spirit from this shrine. The offering of combs and hairpins, along with needle memorial services on February 8 and doll memorial services on March 3, are distinctive aspects of this faith. * Kifune {{ill|Inari Shrine|ja|稲荷社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inari Shrine|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 稲荷神社|1=Inari shrine|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q514480}} – This shrine consolidates the Inari deities once separately enshrined here and in local households. In recent years, a divided spirit has been enshrined from Fushimi Inari Taisha in Kyoto. A Hatsu-uma ritual is held in conjunction with Setsubun on February 3 each year.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/keidai.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Kifune Festival (Main Festival) == [[File:貴船まつり小早船.jpg|alt=Kifune Festival Kobayabune|Thumbnail|Kifune Festival Kobayabune]] [[File:貴船神社の神輿（貴船まつり）.jpg|alt=Kifune Shrine's Mikoshi (Kifune Festival)|Thumbnail|Kifune Shrine's Mikoshi (Kifune Festival)]] {{詳細記事|貴船まつり}} Kifune Festival is the main festival of {{ill|Kifune Shrine|en|Kifune Shrine|qid=Q276779|lt=Kifune Shrine|1=Kifune Shrine}}, a boat festival in which beautifully adorned small swift boats ({{ill|Kobayabune|ja|小早船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kobayabune|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小早|1=kohaya|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q8017865}}) and mikoshi boats—decorated with elaborate floral arrangements and {{ill|fukinagashi|ja|吹き流し|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/fukinagashi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 吹流し|1=Windsock|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q216346}}—are drawn by {{ill|oar-propelled boats|ja|櫂伝馬船|lt=Oar-propelled boat|lt_ja=櫂伝馬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/oar-propelled boats|1=kaidenmasen|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11544651}} in a maritime {{ill|procession|ja|渡御|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/procession|1=Q11561919|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11561919}}. It is held every year on the last {{ill|Saturday|en|Saturday|qid=Q131|lt=Saturday|1=Saturday}} of July and the day before, staged at Manazuru Port and various locations within {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|en|Manazuru, Kanagawa|qid=Q1202786|lt=Manazuru|1=Manazuru, Kanagawa}}.{{Cite web |title=文化財指定 {{!}} 真鶴貴船まつりホームページ【公式】　国指定重要無形民俗文化財　日本三大船祭り |url=https://kibunematsuri.jp/bunkazai.html |website=kibunematsuri.jp |access-date=2024-08-15}} == Ceremonies == * {{ill|New Year Festival|ja|歳旦祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year Festival|1=Saitan-sai|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11546824}} – January 1 * {{ill|Setsubun Festival|ja|節分祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Setsubun Festival|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 節分|1=Setsubun|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q746798}} – Setsubun in February * {{ill|Kinen-sai|en|Kinen-sai|qid=Q118906710|lt=Prayer for a Good Harvest Festival|1=Kinensai}} – February 17 * {{ill|Shōwa Day|en|Shōwa Day|qid=Q1361434|lt=Showa Festival|1=Shōwa Day}} – April 29 * Summer Purification (Nagoshi no Ōharae) – June 30 * {{ill|Main Festival|ja|例大祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Main Festival|ja_comment=fixed the redirect|1=Reisai|2=ja|3=例祭|4=fr|5=Reisai|lt=Reitaisai}} ("{{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q105338690}}") – Last Saturday of July and the day before * {{ill|Niiname-no-Matsuri|en|Niiname-no-Matsuri|qid=Q11501518|lt=Niinamesai|1=Niiname-no-Matsuri}} – November 23 * {{ill|New Year's Eve Ceremony|ja|除夜祭|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/New Year's Eve Ceremony|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} – December 31 * Monthly Festival – On the 1st and 15th of every month{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} == Access == * Alight at {{ill|JR East|ja|JR東日本|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/JR East|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東日本旅客鉄道|1=East Japan Railway Company|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q499071}}, {{ill|Tōkaidō Line|ja|東海道線|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tōkaidō Line|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 東海道本線|1=Tōkaidō Main Line|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q1190152}}, and {{ill|Manazuru Station|en|Manazuru Station|qid=Q865802|lt=Manazuru Station|1=Manazuru Station}}. A 15‐minute walk. * {{ill|Izu Hakone Bus|ja|伊豆箱根バス|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izu Hakone Bus|1=Izuhakone Bus|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11380590}}: Alight at the “Miyamae” bus stop. (Walk 0 minutes.) == References == {{reflist}} == Bibliography == * {{cite book|和書|title=The Great Series of Japanese Geography|volume=Volume 37, New Compilation of Sagami Fudoki, 2nd Volume, Section 32, Ashigarashimo District, Volume 11|chapter=Hayakawashō, Manazuru Village, Kiminomiya Myōjin Shrine|id={{NDLJP|1179210/86}}|ref={{sfnref|新編相模国風土記稿 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社}}|publisher=Yūzankaku|date=1932-8}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.pref.kanagawa.jp/documents/29332/mokuroku.pdf|title=Kanagawa Prefecture Cultural Properties Inventory|website=Kanagawa Prefecture|accessdate=2021-11-6|date=2021-5-1|ref={{sfnref|神奈川県ホームページ}}}} * {{Cite web|和書|url=https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/674|title=Boat Festival of Kifune Shrine|website=National Designated Cultural Properties Database|accessdate=2021-11-6|ref={{sfnref|国指定文化財等データベース}}}} == See Also == * {{ill|Kiminomiya Faith|ja|キノミヤ信仰|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kiminomiya Faith|1=Kinomiya Faith|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11296898}} * {{ill|Kashima Dance|ja|鹿島踊り|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kashima Dance|1=kashima odo|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11677163}} == External Links == * [http://kibunejinja.com/ Kifune Shrine] * [https://kibunematsuri.jp/ Manazuru Kifune Festival] ([https://kibunematsuri.jp/ Kifune Festival Promotion Headquarters]) * [https://kunishitei.bunka.go.jp/heritage/detail/302/674 National Designated Cultural Properties Database (Agency for Cultural Affairs)] * [http://www.town.manazuru.kanagawa.jp/ Manazuru Town] * [https://www.manazuru.net/ Manazuru Navi (General Incorporated Association Manazuru Town Tourism Association)] {{translated page|ja|貴船神社 (真鶴町)|version=104285000|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:貴船神社 (真鶴町)]] {{Commons category|Kibune-jinja (Manazuru, Kanagawa)}} [[co:commons:Category:Kibune-jinja (Manazuru, Kanagawa)]][[ja:貴船神社 (真鶴町)]] [[co:commons:Category:Kibune-jinja (Manazuru, Kanagawa)]][[ja:貴船神社 (真鶴町)]] [[ja:貴船神社 (真鶴町)]] {{Otheruseslist|{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|en|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|qid=Q1193172|lt=Ashigarashimo District|1=Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa}}{{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|en|Manazuru, Kanagawa|qid=Q1202786|lt=Manazuru|1=Manazuru, Kanagawa}}にある'''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社)|Other Kifune Shrines|Kifune Shrine (disambiguation)}} {{infobox religious building | name = Kifune Shrine (Manazuru) | native_name = 貴船神社 | religious_affiliation = {{ill|Kinomiya faith|qid=Q11296898|1=Kinomiya Faith}} | image = File:Kibune jinja manaduru kaidan.JPG | address = {{JPN}}
〒259-0201
{{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}{{ill|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|en|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|qid=Q1193172|lt=Ashigarashimo District|1=Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa}}{{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|en|Manazuru, Kanagawa|qid=Q1202786|lt=Manazuru|1=Manazuru, Kanagawa}}真鶴1117 | coordinates = {{Coord|35.149722|N|139.146111|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | deities = {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}}
{{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}
{{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} | founded = {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|en|Kanpyō (era)|qid=Q1346292|lt=Kanpyō|1=Kanpyō (era)}} 1 (889){{sfn|新編相模国風土記稿 眞鶴村 貴ノ宮明神社}} | shrine_Rank = Former Local Shrine | annual_festival = {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q105338690}} (the last Saturday of July and the day before) | location = {{ill|Manazuru|qid=Q1202786|1=Manazuru, Kanagawa}} | deity = {{ill|Kotoshironushi|qid=Q8009611|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}}; {{ill|Sukunabikona|qid=Q3275687|1=Sukunabikona}}; {{ill|Ōkuninushi|qid=Q276944|1=Ōkuninushi}} }} '''Kifune Shrine''' (貴船神社) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=Shinto shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Kanagawa Prefecture|en|Kanagawa Prefecture|qid=Q127513|lt=Kanagawa Prefecture|1=Kanagawa Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|en|Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa|qid=Q1193172|lt=Ashigarashimo District|1=Ashigarashimo District, Kanagawa}}, and {{ill|Manazuru, Kanagawa|en|Manazuru, Kanagawa|qid=Q1202786|lt=Manazuru|1=Manazuru, Kanagawa}}. It enshrines {{ill|Ōkuninushi|ja|大国主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōkuninushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 大国主}} (おおくにぬしのおおかみ), {{ill|Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto|qid=Q8009611|lt=Kotoshironushi|ja|事代主大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kotoshironushi|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 事代主|1=Kotoshironushi-no-Mikoto}} (ことしろぬしのおおかみ), and {{ill|Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunabikona|lt=Sukunahikona|ja|少彦名大神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Sukunahikona|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to スクナビコナ}} (すくなひこなのおおかみ).{{Cite web |title=貴船神社 |url=https://www.kanagawa-jinja.or.jp/shrine/1210253-000/ |website=神奈川県神社庁 |access-date=2024-08-15 |language=ja}} The main festival, {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q105338690}} (きぶねまつり), has been designated an {{ill|Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|ja|重要無形民俗文化財|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|1=National Important Intangible Folk Cultural Property|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q6573893}}.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/matsuri-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} It is not an enshrinement transferred from the {{ill|Kifune Shrine|en|Kifune Shrine|qid=Q276779|lt=Kifune Shrine|1=Kifune Shrine}} (きふねじんじゃ) of {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|qid=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}} and {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|qid=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}. == History == * {{ill|Kanpyō (era)|en|Kanpyō (era)|qid=Q1346292|lt=Kanpyō|1=Kanpyō (era)}} 1 ({{ill|889|en|889|qid=Q23833|lt=889|1=889}}) – It is said that Hiraio, the ancestral figure of the {{ill|Shake (social class)|en|Shake (social class)|qid=Q11588367|lt=Shrine family|1=Shake (social class)}} Hirai family ({{ill|Ancestor|ja|祖先|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancestor|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 先祖|1=Ancestor|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q402152}}), discovered twelve wooden statues and a {{ill|Letter|en|Letter|qid=Q293618|lt=letter|1=Letter}}—which had appeared offshore near Mitsuishi ({{ill|Manazuru Cape|ja|真鶴岬|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manazuru Cape|1=Cape Manazuru|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q11583649}})—and built a {{ill|shrine building|ja|社殿|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/shrine building|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 神社建築|1=Shinto architecture|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q3080012}}, establishing it as the {{ill|Chinjugami|en|Chinjugami|qid=Q97479976|lt=鎮守|1=Chinjugami}} of the village and naming it Kiminomiya (貴宮) {{ill|Great Deity|ja|大明神|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Great Deity|1=Daimyōjin|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q113497901}}.{{Cite web |title=相州真鶴 貴船神社ウェブサイト |url=http://kibunejinja.com/kibune-history.html |website=kibunejinja.com |access-date=2024-08-15}} * {{ill|Kanbun (era)|en|Kanbun (era)|qid=Q1146228|lt=Kanbun|1=Kanbun (era)}} 12 ({{ill|1678|en|1678|qid=Q7634|lt=1678|1=1678}}) – According to “Sōshū West Part, Westward Line, Manazuru Village Shogakuchō” (Documents of the Gomi Family), it is recorded that as early as then, a shipboard {{ill|prayer ritual|ja|祈祷|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/prayer ritual|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 祈り|1=Prayer|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q40953}}—which may be regarded as the origin of the {{ill|Kifune Festival|ja|貴船まつり|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kifune Festival|1=Kibune Matsuri|comment=changed title based on wikidata|qid=Q105338690}}—was performed at Manazuru
